{{short description|American lecturer, author, art, literature critic and suffragette}} {{Use mdy dates|date=October 2025}} {{Infobox person | honorific_prefix = | name = Mary Hanford Ford | honorific_suffix = | native_name = | native_name_lang = | image = Mary H. Ford, circa 1898.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mary H. Ford, circa 1898, 42 years old | birth_name = Mary Hanford Finney | birth_date = {{Birth date|1856|11|01|mf=y}} | birth_place = [[Meadville, Pennsylvania]], US | death_date = {{Death date and age|mf=yes|1937|02|02|1856|11|01}} | death_place = [[Clearwater, Florida]], US | death_cause = | body_discovered = | resting_place = | resting_place_coordinates = <!-- {{Coord|LAT|LONG|type:landmark|display=inline}} --> | monuments = | other_names = Mary Hanford Finney Ford, Minnie Finnie | citizenship = | education = | alma_mater = | occupation = | years_active = | employer = | organization = | agent = | known_for = Lecturer, author, art and literature critic, a leader in the women's suffrage movement | notable_works = ''Which Wins?'', ''Message of the Mystics'' trilogy, ''The Oriental Rose'' | style = | height = <!-- {{height|m=}} --> | television = | title = | term = | predecessor = | successor = | party = | movement = | opponents = | boards = | criminal_charge = <!-- Criminality parameters should be supported with citations from reliable sources --> | criminal_penalty = | criminal_status = | spouse = Moses Smith Ford | children = 3 | parents = | relatives = | callsign = | awards = | signature = | signature_alt = | signature_size = }}
'''Mary Hanford Ford''' (née '''Finney'''; November 1, 1856 – February 2, 1937) was an American lecturer, author, art and literature critic and a leader in the women's suffrage movement. She reached early notoriety in Kansas at the age of 28 and soon left for [[the Chicago World's Fair]]. She was taken up by the society ladies of the Chicago area who, impressed with her talks on art and literature at the Fair, helped launch her on a new career, initially in Chicago and then across some States. Along the way she was already published in articles and noticed in [[suffrage movement|suffrage meetings]].
In addition to work as an art critic and speaker she wrote a number of books, most prominently a trilogy ''Message of the Mystics''. Circa 1900 to 1902 Ford found the [[Baháʼí Faith]] through [[Sarah Farmer]] and [[Mírzá Abu'l-Faḍl]], and helped form the first community of [[Baháʼí Faith in Greater Boston|Baháʼís in Boston]] where [[Louis Bourgeois (architect)|Louis Bourgeois]], future architect of the first [[Baháʼí House of Worship (Wilmette, Illinois)|Baháʼí House of Worship]] in the West, then joined the religion. In 1907 Ford went on [[Baháʼí pilgrimage]], in 1910 she started writing Baháʼí books such as ''The Oriental Rose'', and traveled with [[ʻAbdu'l-Bahá]] during some of his journeys in various places in Europe and then America.
Ford was blamed for a fiasco among UK suffragists but it was their own violence that got them in trouble. Ford spent the years of [[World War I]] in California following the first Baháʼí International Congress at the [[Panama–Pacific International Exposition]], and then moved back to New York where she spent almost the next 20 years. Often she traveled to Europe for some months of the year and during this period introduced the religion to [[Ugo Giachery]], later a prominent Baháʼí. Also in this period she was censored off a radio broadcast, helped develop the religion's community both in meetings she supported and literary efforts, before reducing her travels and speaking engagements in the early 1930s. She died with her daughter by her bedside in 1937.
==Early days==
===Before 1884=== Mary Hanford Finney was born on November 1, 1856,<ref name=BlueBook>{{cite book |title=Herringshaw's American Blue Book Of Biography |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=UUM6AQAAMAAJ&pg=PA470 |year=1915 |publisher=American Publishers' Association. |page=470}}</ref><ref name=USCensus1900/> (though 1857 is often mentioned,)<ref name=DeathCert/><ref name=1910Passport>{{cite web | url = https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:QV5Y-KJDX | title = Mary Hanford Ford Migration • United States Passport Applications, 1795-1925 | date = July 30, 1910 | publisher = National Archives and Records Administration | via = FamilySearch.org | access-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406151449/https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:QV5Y-KJDX | url-status = live }}{{registration required}}</ref> to a mercantile/banker family<ref name=EllisHistory/><ref name=BWvo7Bio>{{cite book |author=Florence Khanum (Ruhaniyyihi) |title=Baháʼí World |chapter=Mary Hanford Ford (Nov. 1, 1856- Feb. 2, 1937) |volume=7 |edition=BE 93 and 94, April 1936-1938 A.D. |year=1939 |pages=541–2 |chapter-url=http://bahai-library.com/pdf/bw/memoriam_bw_7.pdf |access-date=June 2, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304080729/http://bahai-library.com/pdf/bw/memoriam_bw_7.pdf |url-status=live }}</ref> near [[Meadville, Pennsylvania]]. The family was noted a year later in [[Williamsport, Pennsylvania]].<ref name=KCTobit>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Smith M. Ford dies, Mother of Roland H. Ford formerly lived here |newspaper=Kansas City Times |location=Kansas City, MO |page=5 |date=February 3, 1937 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/122377698/obit-of-bahai-mary-hanford-ford/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 6, 2023 |archive-date=April 6, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230406144011/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/122377698/obit-of-bahai-mary-hanford-ford/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The ''Kansas City Times'' obituary noted she had a brother, A.M. Finney, known from Charleston, West Virginia.<ref name=KCTobit/> That would make her father Asahel Clark Finney, who spent the last working decade of his life as a partner in a Pennsylvania lumber company before moving to Kansas City.<ref name=EllisHistory>{{cite book |author=William Arba Ellis |title=Norwich University, 1819-1911: Her History, Her Graduates, Her Roll of Honor, Pub. by Major-General Grenville M. Dodge ... |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=148pAQAAIAAJ&pg=PA386 |year=1911 |publisher=Capital city Press |page=386}}</ref> Ford's mother was Elizabeth Mary Hanford Edson.<ref name=Who'sWho/><ref name=DeathCert>{{cite web |url = https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:FPWD-69V |title = Mary Hanford Ford Death • Florida Deaths, 1877-1939 |date = February 3, 1937 |publisher = Florida State Board of Health |via = FamilySearch.org |access-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406151450/https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:FPWD-69V |url-status = live }}{{registration required}}</ref> In 1860, the US Census had the family in nearby [[Clearfield, Pennsylvania]], where Finney was a banker with personal assets of about $2000 in 1860 dollars, and he was not the richest man on his block, though also not the poorest. Mary had an older brother Elmer, and a younger sibling Cynthia, and a live-in servant. Mother Mary was 11 years younger than A. C.<ref name=US1860Census>{{cite web | url = https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXPH-7G1 | title = Mary Finney Census • United States Census | date = 1860 | publisher = US Federal Government | via = FamilySearch.org | access-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406151449/https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXPH-7G1 | url-status = live }}{{registration required}}</ref> By 1870, father A. C. was a cashier at a bank with mother and siblings with two livein servants, one a black man, Daniel Owen, from Virginia.<ref>{{cite web |url = https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MZGC-T48 |title = Mary H Finney Census • United States Census |date = July 8, 1870 |via = FamilySearch.org |publisher = US Federal Government |access-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406152203/https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MZGC-T48 |url-status = live }}{{registration required}}</ref> Ford attended a private school<ref name=Who'sWho/><ref>{{cite news |title=Select school for girls |newspaper=The Rutland Daily Globe |location=Rutland, Vermont |page=3 |date=September 14, 1874 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2498494/prof_pullens_school_later_bahai_mary/ |access-date=November 2, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805180815/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2498494/prof_pullens_school_later_bahai_mary/ |url-status=live }}</ref> or seminary and studied art and languages in Burlington, Vermont.<ref name=Who'sWho>{{cite book |author1=John William Leonard |author2=Albert Nelson Marquis |title=Who's who in America |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=J-QDAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA390 |year=1901 |publisher=Marquis Who's Who |page=390}}</ref> It is not clear how or when Mary moved to the Kansas area ahead of her father in 1882.<ref name=EllisHistory/> She married Smith Moses Ford about October 8, 1878, when she was known as Minnie Finnie and was living in [[Winfield, Kansas]].<ref>{{cite news |title=S. M. Ford, of the Kansas City Mail ... |newspaper=Fort Scott Daily Monitor |location=Fort Scott, Kansas |page=4 |date=October 8, 1878 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240205/reporter_s_m_ford_married_minnie_finnie/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184225/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240205/reporter_s_m_ford_married_minnie_finnie/ |url-status=live }}</ref> She was his second wife.<ref name=Truher>{{cite web |author=Nancy Truher |date=January 2, 2005 |editor=Jack Truher |title=Generation No. 5 |work=Report on Ford Family |url=http://gene.truher.net/TBFx/243.html |access-date=May 10, 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303220024/http://gene.truher.net/TBFx/243.html |archive-date=March 3, 2016 |url-status=dead }}</ref> S.M. Ford was a former alderman, teacher, newspaper writer and eventually newspaper publisher.<ref name=Whitneyhistory>{{cite book |author=Carrie Westlake Whitney |title=Kansas City, Missouri: Its History and Its People 1808-1908 |url=https://archive.org/details/kansascitymissou01whit |year=1908 |publisher=S. J. Clarke Publishing Company |pages=[https://archive.org/details/kansascitymissou01whit/page/398 398], 622}}</ref> Their first child, Roland Ford, was born circa 1879.<ref>{{cite news |title=Roland Ford, Sr., Dies: Former Treasurer of the Fred Harvey System Was 71 |newspaper=The Kansas City Public Library |date=November 9, 1950 |url=http://www.kchistory.org/cdm4/item_viewer.php?CISOROOT=/Local&CISOPTR=24767&CISOBOX=1&REC=3 |access-date=May 10, 2015 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303233353/http://www.kchistory.org/cdm4/item_viewer.php?CISOROOT=%2FLocal&CISOPTR=24767&CISOBOX=1&REC=3 |archive-date=March 3, 2016 }}</ref><ref name=USCensus1900/>
It was after this that Mary Hanford Ford became visible as a member of clubs. Circa 1880, Ford was among a circle of women who formed the Friends Council Club in Kansas City, of an intentionally limited number of members, and focused on reviewing the history, literature, philanthropy, and art of early civilizations.<ref name=Whitneyhistory/> For the June 4, 1880, US Census, the family was living on 7th St in Kansas City, Missouri, with her parents and siblings and their own children.<ref>{{cite web | url = https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M6XZ-6V6 | title = Mary H. Ford Census • United States Census | date = June 4, 1880 | publisher = US Federal. Government | via = FamilySearch.org | access-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-date = April 6, 2023 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406183955/https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:M6XZ-6V6 | url-status = live }}{{registration required}}</ref> Father Asahel C. and husband Smith M. were in real estate, as was her brother Elmer. There were also three black servants in the house and five boarders. Circa 1882, Ford was visible entertaining visitors.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Geo. S. Brown has returned ... |newspaper=The Daily Commonwealth |location=Topeka, Kansas |page=4 |date=February 19, 1882 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240069/mrs_s_m_ford_aka_mrs_smith_moses/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184350/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240069/mrs_s_m_ford_aka_mrs_smith_moses/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Another early club Ford was involved in was the Social Science Club; she was visible speaking at a regional conference in 1884.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Social Science club at Kansas City |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, Kansas |via=Newspapers.com |date=May 6, 1884 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240125/social_science_club_1883_meeting_i/ |page=4, [https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240117/social_science_club_1883_meeting_ii/ continued] |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304212733/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2240125/social_science_club_1883_meeting_i/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Ford's daughter Lynette was born on 1886.<ref name=USCensus1900/>
===Rising tide of visibility===
1884 and 1885 was Ford's first known writing appeared in print. A short article, "What is Wanted", was fairly widely published including Illinois,<ref>* {{cite news |last = Ford |first = Mary H. |title = What is Wanted |newspaper = The Streator Free Press |location = Streator, IL |page = 3 |date = August 9, 1884 |url = https://www.newspapers.com/clip/122375084/later-bahai-mary-hanford-ford-article/ |via = Newspapers.com |access-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406185059/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/122375084/later-bahai-mary-hanford-ford-article/ |url-status = live }} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Centralia Daily Sentinel |location=Centralia, IL |page=3 |date=October 10, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/centralia/centralia-daily-sentinel/1884/10-10/page-3 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304040031/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/centralia/centralia-daily-sentinel/1884/10-10/page-3 |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Monmouth Evening Gazette |location=Monmouth, IL |page=2 |date=November 8, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/monmouth/monmouth-evening-gazette/1884/11-08/page-2 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304030800/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/monmouth/monmouth-evening-gazette/1884/11-08/page-2 |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}}</ref> Indiana,<ref>* {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=The Fort Wayne Journal-Gazette |location=Fort Wayne, IN |page=7 |date=August 10, 1884 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163856/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805181434/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163856/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Fort Wayne Gazette |location=Fort Wayne, IN |page=7 |date=August 10, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/indiana/fort-wayne/fort-wayne-gazette/1884/08-10/page-7 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=November 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231107161859/https://newspaperarchive.com/fort-wayne-gazette-aug-10-1884-p-7/ |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=New Harmony Register |location=New Harmony, IN |page=2 |date=October 31, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/indiana/new-harmony/new-harmony-register/1884/10-31/page-2 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=November 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231107161858/https://newspaperarchive.com/new-harmony-register-oct-31-1884-p-2/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Iowa,<ref>*{{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Cedar Rapids Evening Gazette - October 9, 1884 |location=Cedar Rapids, IA |page=5 |date=August 9, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/cedar-rapids/cedar-rapids-evening-gazette/1884/10-09/page-5 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304030827/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/cedar-rapids/cedar-rapids-evening-gazette/1884/10-09/page-5 |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=The Perry Pilot |location=Perry, IA |page=2 |date=January 3, 1885 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164691/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805180523/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164691/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Perry Pilot |location=Perry, IA |page=2 |date=January 3, 1885 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/perry/perry-pilot/1885/01-03/page-2 |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304025337/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/perry/perry-pilot/1885/01-03/page-2 |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Alden Times |location=Alden, IA |page=6 |date=January 16, 1885 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/alden/alden-times/1885/01-16/page-6 |via=NewspaperArchive.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304002307/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/iowa/alden/alden-times/1885/01-16/page-6 |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}}</ref> Kansas,<ref>* {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS |page=3 |date=August 15, 1884 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163864/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304211252/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163864/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS |page=3 |date=October 17, 1884 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164685/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805183316/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163864/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Maine,<ref>{{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=Biddeford Daily Journal |location=Biddeford, ME |page=4 |date=August 20, 1884 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/maine/biddeford/biddeford-daily-journal/1884/08-20/page-4 |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=November 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231107161858/https://newspaperarchive.com/biddeford-daily-journal-aug-20-1884-p-4/ |url-status=live }}{{subscription required}}</ref> Ohio,<ref>* {{cite news |author=Mary H Ford |title=What is wanted |newspaper=The Hicksville News |location=Hicksville, OH |page=3 |date=October 2, 1884 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164654/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805183510/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164654/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |last = Ford |first = Mary H. |title = What is Wanted |newspaper = Belmont Chronicle Saint |location = Clairsville, OH |page = 4 |date = January 1, 1885 |via = Newspapers.com |url = https://www.newspapers.com/clip/6267151/article-by-later-bahai-mary-hanford/ |access-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406185059/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/6267151/article-by-later-bahai-mary-hanford/ |url-status = live }}</ref> and Pennsylvania.<ref>{{cite news |last = Ford |first = Mary H. |title = What is Wanted |newspaper = The Hazleton Sentinel |location = Hazleton, Pennsylvania |page = 1 |date = August 21, 1884 |via = Newspapers.com |url = https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5086290/later-bahai-mary-hanford-ford-about/ |access-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-date = April 6, 2023 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20230406191636/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5086290/later-bahai-mary-hanford-ford-about/ |url-status = live }}</ref> Ford pointed out that, in the past, people only needed or wanted food and shelter, but even though some still lacked these basic needs, growing spiritual needs were developed that required a home that provided a quiet and private space to develop one's individuality, away from gossip and criticism, a home should be a place where children can maintain their innocence and not grow up too quickly. She criticizes most Americans for having mere places to stay rather than true homes that foster love, thoughts, and morals and fail to recognize their home as a place of spiritual importance, with material possessions and obligations taking precedence over religious beliefs. "But the idea of a heart center, where love is cherished, thoughts are fostered and morals expanded, is apparently unheard of in their philosophy. Materfamilias goes to church on Sunday without an idea that she is leaving the holiest temple behind her, of which she is high priestess, and if you should dissect her brain you would find that in the corner devoted to 'necessities' the strata of clothes, cooks, sewing girls and roast turkey fill up all but the smallest crevice consciously set aside for religious belief and church membership." That same period, after having been an atheist for most of her life,<ref name=Whitehead>{{cite book |last=Whitehead |first=O. Z. |author-link=O. Z. Whitehead |title=Some Baha'is to Remember |publisher=George Ronald |date=1983 |pages=145–152 |url=http://grbooks.com/george-ronald-publisher-books/heritage-books/some-bahais-to-remember-1317293329 |isbn=0-85398-148-5 |access-date=June 2, 2015 |archive-date=May 17, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160517175014/http://grbooks.com/george-ronald-publisher-books/heritage-books/some-bahais-to-remember-1317293329 |url-status=dead }}</ref> Ford became a [[Kardecist spiritism|Spiritist]] and a believer in the [[afterlife]] when her father died in Kansas City.<ref name=EllisHistory/>
In July 1888, Ford published the instructive article "Mrs. Diaz and the Woman's Exchange Idea" in a periodical.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary H. Ford contributes ... |newspaper=Lawrence Daily Journal |location=Lawrence, KS |page=4 |date=July 18, 1888 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164695/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214008/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164695/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Her last child was born in September 1888,<ref name=USCensus1900/> the same month an article in ''The York Daily'' describes her as being "recognized in the west as an authority on literary matters", and she was quoted saying, of the role of the US President's wife, "...By countenancing the suffrage movement she could make it more fashionable ..."<ref>{{cite news |title=If the President's wife |newspaper=The York Daily |location=York, PA |page=3 |date=September 24, 1888 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164707/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_in/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214008/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164707/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_in/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In October, she was the only woman, among four vice presidents, elected for a newly founded Missouri and Kansas authors' organization.<ref>{{cite news |title=Author's meeting |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS |page=7 |date=October 21, 1888 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164713/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214047/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164713/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite journal |title=Notes; A club of Western authors and artists has been started ...|journal=The Critic: A Weekly Review of Literature and the Arts |volume=253 |page=224 |date=November 3, 1888 |issn= <!--|access-date=May 18, 2015-->}}</ref> That year was also Ford's last birth.<ref>{{cite web |title=Gareth H. Ford - 1930 Census Record |website=mooseroots.com |url=http://us-census.mooseroots.com/l/326144414/Gareth-H-Ford |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150418174418/http://us-census.mooseroots.com/l/326144414/Gareth-H-Ford |url-status=dead |archive-date=April 18, 2015 |access-date=May 10, 2015 }}</ref>
In 1889 she was an officer of the Western Authors and Artists Club (WCCA) of Kansas City,<ref>{{cite news |title=Literature and art; The third semi-annual convention of the W. A. A. C. in Kansas City |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS |page=8 |date=September 24, 1889 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164723/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_officer/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805185123/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164723/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_officer/ |url-status=live }}</ref> writing various articles including a series for [[Edward Bellamy]]'s ''The Nationalist'',<ref>* {{cite journal |author=M. H. Ford |title=The Message (poem) |journal=The Nationalist |volume=1 |issue=4 |page=105 |date=August 1889 |url=https://archive.org/stream/TheNationalist-Volume1-1889/Nationalist-Volume-1#page/n124/mode/1up |access-date=April 28, 2015}} * {{cite journal |author=Mary H Ford |title=An artful incident |journal=The Nationalist |volume=1 |issue=5 |pages=141–144 |date=September 1889 |url=https://archive.org/stream/TheNationalist-Volume1-1889/Nationalist-Volume-1#page/n164/mode/1up |access-date=April 28, 2015}} * {{cite journal |author=Mary H. Ford |title=A Feminine Iconoclast |journal=The Nationalist |volume=1 |issue=7 |pages=252–257 |date=November 1889 |url=https://archive.org/stream/TheNationalist-Volume1-1889/Nationalist-Volume-1#page/n283/mode/1up |access-date=April 28, 2015}}</ref> an article reviewing a book on artists for ''[[The Dial]]'',<ref>{{cite journal |author=Mary H. Ford |title=Painters, Portraits of Famous |journal=The Dial |volume=10 |issue=116 |pages=212–¿ |date=December 1889 |issn= <!--|access-date=April 28, 2015-->}}</ref> and then a series of stories for ''Wide Awake'',<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford, of Kansas City, ... |newspaper=Lawrence Daily Journal |location=Lawrence, KS |page=2 |date=December 19, 1889 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164728/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_articles/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 7, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190407030024/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164728/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_articles/ |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Daniel Lothrop|a children's magazine]], a series that continued in 1890.<ref>{{cite news |title=The April ''Wide Awake'', ... |newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY |pages=17 |date=March 23, 1890 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164735/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 7, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190407025949/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164735/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
In 1891, now a member of the Kansas City Art association, she took part in the opening of an exhibit.<ref>{{cite news |title=Art Exhibition |newspaper=The Leavenworth Times |location=Leavenworth, KS |page=4 |date=March 29, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164742/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_at_art/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184203/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164742/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_at_art/ |url-status=live }}</ref> This same year she wrote the book, ''Which will win?'' or ''Which wins?'',<ref>{{cite news |title=The bright and capable members ... |newspaper=Fort Scott Daily Monitor |location=Fort Scott, KS |page=1 |date=May 9, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164753/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 7, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190407030020/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164753/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_article/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and co-wrote a play. "Mary H. Ford ... says in her preface to the book that there are many men, like [[Richard Wagner|Wagner]]'s [[Parsifal]], whose eyes turn inward, who feel the sufferings of others so vividly that they will turn their backs upon worldly prosperity and sacrifice all worldly profit for the good of their fellow-creatures. With such men lies the possibility of the race for real reform, and they represent a proportion of humanity much larger now than at any other time, she thinks. ... "<ref>{{cite journal |title=Mary, H. Ford, the author of 'Which Wins' ...|journal=The Critic: A Weekly Review of Literature and the Arts |volume=16 |issue=394 |page=29 |date=July 18, 1891 |url= <!--|access-date=April 28, 2015-->}}</ref> The book was dedicated to the [[Farmers' Alliance]] and covers life on farms and the effect mortgage systems had on it,<ref>* {{cite news |title=The Times has received ... |newspaper=The Times |location=Clay Center, KS |page=2 |date=May 14, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164762/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304100017/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164762/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=A story for the times |newspaper=Syracuse Evening Herald |location=Syracuse, New York |page=3 |date=May 15, 1891 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-york/syracuse/syracuse-evening-herald/1891/05-15/page-3 |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304025518/http://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-york/syracuse/syracuse-evening-herald/1891/05-15/page-3 |url-status=live }}</ref> and specifically examined the ways a woman's inheritance could be taken away.<ref>{{cite book |author1=Barbara A. Bardes |author2=Suzanne Gossett |title=Declarations of Independence: Women and Political Power in Nineteenth-century American Fiction |url=https://archive.org/details/declarationsofin00bard |url-access=registration |year=1990 |publisher=Rutgers University Press |isbn=978-0-8135-1500-7 |page=[https://archive.org/details/declarationsofin00bard/page/180 180]}}</ref> It was well reviewed locally, in Chicago and beyond.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Which wins? |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=10 |date=May 16, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164795/review_of_later_bahai_mary_hanford/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805182928/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164795/review_of_later_bahai_mary_hanford/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=For the Farmers' Alliance - Which wins? Mary H. Ford |newspaper=Harrisburg Telegraph |location=Harrisburg, PA |page=2 |date=May 16, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164800/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 6, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306095325/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164800/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Which wins? Mary H Ford ... |newspaper=The San Francisco Call |location=San Francisco, CA |page=1 |date=May 17, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164809/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 6, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306085559/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164809/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Through the courtesy ... |newspaper=The Advocate |location=Meriden, KS |page=1 |date=May 20, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164817/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304065512/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164817/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_book/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=C. S. Gleed |title=A brilliant book |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS |page=2 |date=May 31, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164845/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_detailed/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304092735/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164845/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_detailed/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite journal |title="Which Wins?" is a cleverly told story ...|journal=The Universalist Quarterly and General Review |volume=28 |page=378 |date=July 1891 |url= <!--|access-date=May 10, 2015-->}}</ref> From a Boston review: "although the work of an unknown author, is regarded ... as likely to arouse attention. Nothing is known of Mrs. Mary H. Ford of Kansas City, ... (but) a woman of extended acquaintance both in political and social circles. ... The didactic moral of the book is interwoven with a love-tale, the idea evidently being to reach the story reading public in the same way that Bellamy reached the public with his 'Looking Backward'."<ref>{{cite journal |author=Charles E L Wingate |title=Boston Letter |journal=The Critic: A Weekly Review of Literature and the Arts |volume=15 |issue=384 |page=252 |date=May 9, 1891 |issn= <!--|access-date=May 10, 2015-->}}</ref> However another reviewer said "If the 'labor question' could be settled by writing weak or sensational novels concerning it, all difficulties would speedily vanish ... ''Which Wins'', by Mary H Ford ... would be likely to interest the farmer more if it had less about Parsifal in it."<ref>{{cite journal |title=Fiction: the "labor question" could be settled ...|journal=The Literary World |volume=22 |issue=13 |page=224 |date=June 20, 1891 }}</ref> A letter-to-the-editor campaign by George Ward supported it in several newspapers.<ref>* {{cite news |author=George Ward |title=Sound advice |newspaper=The Daily Times |location=New Philadelphia, OH |page=3 |date=May 28, 1891 required}}</ref>
One reviewer profiled Ford herself:
<blockquote>Mrs Ford is an Eastern woman by birth, and has spent a great deal of her life in Boston, where she is well known and esteemed for her superior capabilities. ... She possesses one of the finest libraries west of the Mississippi, which is rich in art works, rare translations from the Sanscrit, and the choicest editions of the poets. Her education is thorough to a remarkable degree ... Aside from her literary ability, Mrs. Ford is a practical newspaper woman, and can pen a political editorial with the crisp conciseness of a vertebral chief-of-staff. When that historical attempt to run all the emancipated slaves into Kansas was made, Mrs. Ford accepted a commission from the ''New York Tribune'' to investigate the matter, and her series of caustic and exhaustive letters attained a national importance.<ref>{{cite journal |title=Gossip of authors and writers |journal=Current Literature |volume=8 |issue=3 |pages=36–347 (see pp. 344–345) |date=November 1891 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=umn.31951d01799568p;view=1up;seq=992 |access-date=May 12, 2015 |archive-date=December 17, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191217034926/https://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=umn.31951d01799568p;view=1up;seq=992 |url-status=live }}</ref></blockquote>
The new play was called ''The Syndicate'' and it opened in November 1891.<ref>{{cite news |title=A new play by a Kansas City writer ... |newspaper=Arkansas City Daily Traveler |location=Arkansas City, KS |page=2 |date=November 19, 1891 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164864/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184158/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164864/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }}</ref> It was a local success by early 1892 and positives review were carried in various places<ref>* {{cite news |title=The Syndicate |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=4 |date=January 29, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164895/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304112008/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164895/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Play by women - instantaneous hit by a recent production |newspaper=The Saint Paul Globe |location=Saint Paul, MN |page=1 |date=January 29, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164879/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805183212/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164879/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=A great hit - Kansas political life as seen on the stage at Kansas City |newspaper=The Leavenworth Times |location=Leavenworth, KS |page=1 |date=January 29, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164869/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304094832/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164869/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=A great hit - Kansas political life as seen on the stage at Kansas City |newspaper=The Leavenworth Weekly Times |location=Leavenworth, KS |page=7 |date=February 4, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164897/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184156/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164895/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }}</ref> but its sympathies with farmers and the Farmers' Alliance were controversial to some.<ref>* {{cite news |title=More calamity |newspaper=The Kansas City Gazette |location=Kansas City, KS |page=2 |date=January 29, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164888/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304083951/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164888/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=More calamity |newspaper=The Kansas City Gazette |location=Kansas City, KS |page=2 |date=February 4, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164910/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805185113/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164888/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_coauthor/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
That spring Ford hosted a tent of her own at a local fair.<ref>* {{cite news |author=May Belleville Brown |title=A party of twenty-five ladies ... |newspaper=The Saline County Journal |location=Salina, KS |page=1 |date=July 7, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164927/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304221620/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164927/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |author=May Belleville Brown |title=(continued) |newspaper=The Saline County Journal |location=Salina, KS |page=1 |date=July 7, 1892 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2241091/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |via=Newspapers.com |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184240/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164927/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_among/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
===Transition to Chicago (1893–1894)===
In March 1893, a man sued Ford's husband $50,000 for having "alienated the affections of his wife and caused her to desert him in 1887" and also for having abducted the couple's daughter in 1888.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Ex-Editor Ford sued for $50,000 |newspaper=The Evening Kansan |location=Newton, KS |page=1 |date=March 15, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286193/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805181734/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286193/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=An obliging husband |newspaper=Independence Daily Reporter |location=Independence, KS |page=1 |date=March 17, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2239860/mary_hanford_fords_tobe_exhusband/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304200838/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2239860/mary_hanford_fords_tobe_exhusband/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=An obliging husband |newspaper=Lawrence Weekly World |location=Lawrence, KS |page=1 |date=March 16, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286202/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 6, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306102016/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286202/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Sensation in Kansas City |newspaper=The Sedalia Democrat |location=Sedalia, MO |page=6 |date=April 2, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286209/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805181734/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2286193/smith_moses_ford_husband_of_mary/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A week later, replacing a previously announced speaker, Ford gave an hour-long address at a mass meeting of the local [[suffrage movement]] to encourage women's interest in the upcoming municipal election.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs Mary H. Ford ... |newspaper=The Kansas City Gazette |location=Kansas City, KS |page=3 |date=March 22, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164966/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Women at the tabernacle - Mrs Ford addresses an interested audience on equal suffrage |newspaper=The Kansas City Gazette |location=Kansas City, KS |page=3 |date=March 23, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164974/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_followup/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 Women's Suffrage, including one detailing the accomplishments the movement had effected in Missouri.<ref>{{cite news |title=Equal suffragists organized - prepared to do effective work for women's rights |newspaper=The Kansas City Gazette |location=Kansas City, KS |page=1 |date=March 30, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2164980/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184347/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353740/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In May she was again an officer of the |url-status=live }}</ref> In June she attended [[the Chicago World's Fair]], which ran from May 1, 1893, to October 30, 1893, where she was seen as a "strong believer in women" and "American art"<ref>* {{cite news |title=June programs for women |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |pages=3 |date=May 29, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436729/mary_h_hanford_ford_at_womens/ |access-date=May 18, 2015 |archive-date=November 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231107161900/https://www.newspapers.com/article/chicago-tribune-later-bahai-mary-h-han/2436729/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=One day at the Exposition |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=7 |date=June 28, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165019/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 12, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200312010557/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436729/later-bahai-mary-h-hanford-ford-at/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and gave a talk on "Woman in Art Today".<ref>{{cite news |title=World's Columbian Exposition news; Future events |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |pages=1 |date=June 21, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436747/later_bahai_mary_h_hanford_ford_at/ |access-date=May 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 Exposition and the strength of her support for American art was still echoing in October when she again presented, this time on "The Old Masters and the New" (in art)<ref>{{cite news |title=Old masters and the new |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=7 |date=October 24, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165030/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805183257/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165030/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and published an article in the ''Chicago Tribune'' covering impressionism at the Expo in October.<ref>{{cite news |author=Mary H. Ford |title=The Impressionists Pictures at the Fair |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |pages=6 |date=October 8, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436779/later_bahai_mary_h_hanford_ford/ |access-date=May 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 name=CTHProfile>* {{cite news |title=Of interest to woman and home |newspaper=The Utica Daily Observer |location=Utica, NY |page=3 |date=November 2, 1898 as an avid supporter of [[impressionism]] and American art, and was offering classes in her home in Chicago. She also hosted an exhibition in her home in December that was well received in the news<ref>{{cite news |title=Another picture with a history is one seen yesterday ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=15 |date=December 31, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165060/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_hosts/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184326/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165060/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_hosts/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and announced a series of lectures for the coming year for the "Arché Club"<ref>{{cite news |title=The Arche Club ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=15 |date=December 31, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165052/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_has/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184300/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165052/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_has/ |url-status=live }}</ref> suggesting she had already traveled in Europe.<ref>{{cite news |title=Archi Club's series of lectures |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |pages=26 |date=December 17, 1893 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436824/later_bahai_mary_h_hanford_ford/ |access-date=May 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805181222/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2436824/later_bahai_mary_h_hanford_ford/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The club would soon be a primary vehicle for Ford's talks.
In January 1894, she presented "Art and the Revolution" to the [[Sons of the American Revolution]] chapter in Chicago. "It was something entirely original, and was in itself almost a history of that time of struggle" and noted particularly [[Benjamin West]] and his painting ''[[The Death of Wolfe]]'' remarking that this was the first American artist to paint what people really wore and not classical garments.<ref>* {{cite news |title=On Tuesday evening next ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=27 |date=January 21, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165149/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805182748/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165149/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Sons of the revolution - presentation of a Flag to the society - plaintera of early times |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=January 24, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165156/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184303/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165149/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In February she gave a talk reviewing sculpture for the Arché club. The article noted that one day at the Exposition she had spoken during the absence of a planned speaker: "Mrs. Ford did not look as though she would set the world on fire with her eloquence ... but she certainly did inspire the audience, and since that memorable day she has made great strides forward."<ref>{{cite news |title=Next Thursday afternoon ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=27 |date=February 18, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165164/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214055/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165164/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> About a week later she gave a talk on French art: "She showed a thorough acquaintance with her subject, and her talk was replete with incidents in the lives of the artists spoken of, as well as thorough criticism of their works."<ref>{{cite news |title=Will take them all in - the Art Institute decides upon a plan to extend its influence |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=13 |date=February 24, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165171/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805180904/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165171/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A month later she was doing a talk for the benefit of the University Creche ([[Day care]]) of the Children's Aide Society that featured [[Dante Gabriel Rossetti]] and [[Edward Burne-Jones]].<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give a talk ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=March 21, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165174/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304072837/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165174/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=An artistic affair - Entertainment at the home of Mrs. Strahan in Kenwood |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=6 |date=March 25, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165178/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184208/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165174/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The end of that week she gave a talk at [[Hull House]] on English art.<ref>{{cite news |title=It is delightful to know ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=27 |date=March 25, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165181/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805180703/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165181/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A couple weeks later she gave a talk for the Arché club on [[Stained glass#Innovations in the United States|American stained glass]].<ref>{{cite news |title=The reception tended ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=11 |date=April 15, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165189/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 classes were announced in May while she gave a talk at a private collection.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Potter Palmer will ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=27 |date=May 13, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165238/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 weeks.<ref>{{cite news |title=A varied program of work |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=42 |date=May 20, 1894 artists' lineages was to be published and was noted coming out of Boston.<ref>{{cite news |title=Literary Gossip; The same house will publish ... |newspaper=Boston Post |location=Boston, MA |page=4 |date=September 27, 1894 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/massachusetts/boston/boston-post/1894/09-27/page-4 |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 by Ford was outlined for Fridays by the Arché club.<ref>{{cite news |title=The opening reception of the sixth season ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |pages=12 |date=October Inspiration'' would be published,<ref>{{cite news |title=Out of the art world comes an author ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=31 |date=November 18, 1894 book |author=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Otto's Inspiration |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=d6M_AAAAYAAJ |year=1895 |publisher=S. C. Griggs}}</ref> The new series of talks was noted in a few articles: On [[Jean-François Millet|Millet]],<ref>{{cite news |title=The peculiar pathetic life of the famous French ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=December 1, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2301628/the_peculiar_pathetic_life_of_the/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805183500/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2301628/the_peculiar_pathetic_life_of_the/ |url-status=live }}</ref> American art,<ref>{{cite news |title=Catholic Women's League - Literary and musical entertainment at the Columbus Club |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |pages=15 |date=December 9, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165628/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184318/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165628/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and about a week later on French illustrators,<ref>{{cite news |title=Rare lace and art - Arche Club's afternoon devoted to dainty affairs |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=December 15, 1894 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165638/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805180705/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165638/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> for the Arché club. So in 1894 she had taught classes sometimes weekly and given talks noted in newspapers somewhere between monthly and weekly for the year and in the Fall finished a book about to come out. 1895 would see a serious rise in the reports of her talks.
==Chicago and a new career==
=== 1895 – A powerful year === In early January, Ford gave lectures on [[Honoré de Balzac]], [[Édouard Manet]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will lecture ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=January 7, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165643/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805230048/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165643/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and on ''[[Trilby (novel)|Trilby]]'', a recently published novel.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Ford will talk ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=9 |date=January 4, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353744/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304064735/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353744/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Lecture on Trilby |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=9 |date=January 10, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353747/later_bahai_mary_hanford_fords/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805231935/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353744/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk/ |url-status=live }}</ref> She initiated a series of classes on "English Art",<ref>{{cite news |title=The first of a series ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=January 8, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165648/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214052/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165648/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and a second series on Shakespeare, meeting about every two weeks.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Young Ladie's Aid Society ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=6 |date=January 12, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165654/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 6, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170806000857/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165654/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Meanwhile, individual talks continued into mid-January (even naming one of the rooms after her) for the Arché club.<ref>* {{cite news |title=The Arché club holds ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=6 |date=January 12, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165657/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304104419/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165657/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=The Arche Club ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=January 16, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165679/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_honored/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 6, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160306081406/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165679/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_honored/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=The Arche Club (continued) |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=January 16, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165675/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_honored/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805223356/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165657/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_to_give/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A few days later she gave a talk on [[George Sand]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Lecture on George Sand - Arche Club members listen to Mrs. Mary H. Ford and they enjoy a dance |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=7 |date=January 19, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165690/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184213/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165690/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and Trilby,<ref>{{cite news |title=Tuesday evening members of the Arch Club ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=22 |date=January 20, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165702/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184205/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165702/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and another talk in the next week.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=14 |date=January 20, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165708/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805223419/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165708/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> There are yet three more talks noted before the end of January: [[Jean-François Millet|Millet]] before the Chicago South Side Women's club,<ref>{{cite news |title=Lecture on Millet |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=January 23, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165714/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805220739/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165714/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Édouard Manet]] and [[impressionism]] for the Arché club,<ref>{{cite news |title=The Arche Club held its weekly ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=7 |date=January 26, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165722/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214119/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165722/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and [[Rosa Bonheur]] for her class.<ref>{{cite news |title="Rosa Bonbeur and the Women Painters of France"... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=10 |date=January 28, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165742/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805222755/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165742/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
February continued the pace. She began a series before the new Chicago Culture Club at the [[Church of the Epiphany (Chicago)]],<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford is to give a course ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=12 |date=February 8, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165749/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304094053/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165749/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=The regular meeting of the Chicago Culture Club ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=February 10, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165761/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805231855/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165749/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and less than a week later gave a talk on Hamlet,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. later she gave a talk for the Menoken Club.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Menoken Club's popularity ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=February 20, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165786/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805231943/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165808/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> There was an individual talk on March 10 on [[The Tempest]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will lecture ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=4 |date=March 10, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353776/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805231854/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353776/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> followed by a "salon day" for the Arché club.<ref>{{cite news |title="Salon Day" at the Arche club |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=9 |date=March 26, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353778/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805222819/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2353778/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Then she announced another series of lectures and continued the individual talks. The first series of talks during some recitals at individual's homes on Beethoven, Wagner, and Schumann each a week apart in what is now the [[Prairie Avenue District]].<ref>{{cite news |title=A series of four recitals ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=March 21, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166187/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_a/ |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166231/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166264/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166298/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 had outgrown the space of rooms in churches.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs Mary H. Ford will speak ... |newspaper=Sterling Daily Gazette |location=Sterling, IL |page=8 |date=April 4, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166322/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214056/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166322/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Nevertheless, benefits were still held in churches, such as for the [[YWCA]] with a talk about [[John Ruskin]], also a [[Spiritualism (movement)|Spiritualist]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give a talk ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=April 5, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166346/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214038/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166346/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Another publicized talk at a home was on "French Women of the Salon".<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. J. W. Nicholson ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=7 |date=April 6, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166365/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184222/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166365/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> She made a trip outside Chicago to [[Sterling, IL]] for a talk the next day.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford arrived ... |newspaper=Sterling Daily Gazette |location=Sterling, IL |page=8 |date=April 6, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166356/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=March 12, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200312011007/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166356/later-bahai-mary-hanford-ford-arrives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> But she was back in time to give a talk in a week,<ref>{{cite news |title=The Chicago Culture Club ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=8 |date=April 14, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166377/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214050/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166377/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and in a few days was giving a new talk |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166416/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805222804/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166416/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In May she gave two lectures.<ref>{{cite news |title="Women of the French Salons"... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=6 |date=May 19, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166446/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805222954/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166446/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Entertainments announced for the Diversion of the Fashionable World |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=30 |date=May 26, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166460/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 6, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170806000913/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166460/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A repeat of one of the recitals comes in early June, held at the [[Hotel Windermere (Chicago)]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Miss S. Ella Wood will sing ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=37 |date=June 9, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166473/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214056/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166473/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> In a new turn of events, Ford challenged the ''Inter Ocean'' art critic on the virtues of [[impressionism]] - the critic opens with a line from Ford's letter to her "After having told the world what you see in Manet's pictures, don't you think you should do the graceful thing and tell what the artist sees?". Together they reviewed an exhibition,<ref>{{cite news |title=The World of Art |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=35 |date=June 16, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166502/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406214111/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166502/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford/ |url-status=live }}</ref> out of which the critic concedes "... and truth to tell (Manet's defender) took home the palm."
{{Location map+|United States Chicago Greater|width=400|float=right|caption=Known locations of talks of Ford in 1895|places= {{Location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.70735|long=-87.61620}} {{Location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.85431|long=-87.62232}} {{location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.87100|long=-87.62423}} {{location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.79000|long=-87.63039}} {{location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.79361|long=-87.58472}} {{Location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.87861|long=-87.66639}} {{location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.88142|long=-87.66670}} {{location map~|United States Chicago Greater|label=|position=top|lat=41.88270|long=-87.62974}} }}
In August, Ford presented on Wagner's trilogy at [[Green Acre Baháʼí School|Green Acre]], in [[Eliot, Maine]].<ref>* {{cite journal |title=Notes & Announcements; The Summerschool at Greenacre |journal=The Arena |volume=13 |issue=2 |pages=15–18 |date=July 1895 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=AOk7AQAAMAAJ&pg=PR18-IA30 |access-date=May 19, 2015 |archive-date=November 7, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231107162008/https://books.google.com/books?id=AOk7AQAAMAAJ&pg=PR18-IA30#v=onepage&q&f=false |url-status=live }} * {{cite news |title=Life in Boston temporarily transferred to charming "Greenacre" |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |pages=10 |date=August 3, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/652954/1895_aug_03_miss_farmers_work/ |access-date=May 10, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805222808/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/652954/1895_aug_03_miss_farmers_work/ |url-status=live }}</ref> This was an early link in events that would become important for Ford in 1901.
In September, with the Arché club, she launched a series for the Winter on "Modern German Art, Literature, and Music",<ref>{{cite news |title=The Arché club will ... |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL |page=16 |date=September 11, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166512/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=August 5, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805235157/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166512/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> and directly after announcing the series she did a talk at a Wagner recital.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Arche Club will ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=30 |date=September 15, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166518/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184110/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166518/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Two other Clubs, the Harvard Club and Home Club, announced she would lead a literature group of each.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will be ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=30 |date=September 22, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166553/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_works_in/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184219/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166553/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_works_in/ |url-status=live }}</ref> A rather large crowd of women listened to her for a talk entitled "The Colonial Painters, [[Benjamin West|West]], [[John Trumbull|Trumbull]], [[John Singleton Copley|Copley]], and [[Gilbert Stuart|Stuart]]" in early October,<ref>{{cite news |title=Nearly 150 women attending ... |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=5 |date=October 3, 1895 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166572/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |access-date=April 18, 2015 |archive-date=April 6, 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190406184150/https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166572/later_bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives/ |url-status=live }}</ref> while the recitals on German art and talks continued.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Arche Club |newspaper=The Inter Ocean |location=Chicago, IL |page=6 |date=October 5, 1895
