|url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/berkeley/berkeley-daily-gazette/1916/04-29/page-7 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Near mid-summer Ford was in Portland Oregon giving a talks - one in the public library on world peace with William Hoar,<ref>{{cite news |title= Peace meeting tonight |newspaper= Oregonian |location =Portland, OR|page=(section 3), 7 |date =June 11, 1916 }}</ref> and another before the local art association.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Ford to speak ...|newspaper=Oregonian |location =Portland, OR|page=13 |date =June 14, 1916 }}</ref> The following July she was in Denver giving a talk on peace for the local women's club.<ref>{{cite news |title=Large army would menace nation, says fair speaker |newspaper=Denver Rocky Mountain News |location =Denver, Colorado |page=5 |date =July 11, 1916 }}</ref>
===Traveling=== {{Location map+|United States|width=300|float=left|caption=Early travels giving talks as a Baháʼí|places= {{Location map~|United States|label=[[Washington, D.C.]]|position=bottom|lat=38.9047 |long=-77.0164}} {{location map~|United States|label= [[Wilmette, Illinois|Wilmette]]|position=left|lat=42.0772|long=-87.7236}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Boston, Massachusetts|Boston]]|position=top|lat=42.3581|long=-71.0636}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[New York City|New York]]|position=left|lat=40.7128 |long=-74.0058}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Topeka, Kansas|Topeka]]|position=right|lat=39.0558 |long=-95.6894}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Oakland, California|Oakland]]|position=bottom|lat=37.8044 |long=-122.2708}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Portland, Oregon|Portland]]|position=top|lat=45.52 |long=-122.6819}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Denver, Colorado|Denver]]|position=left|lat=39.7619 |long=-104.8811}} }} By September 1916, Ford had begun traveling. First she spoke before the Baháʼís of [[Washington, D.C.]] on peace,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford ...|newspaper=The Washington Post |location=Washington, DC|page=5 |date=September 28, 1916 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163368/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk_i/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=(continued) |newspaper=The Washington Post |location=Washington, DC|page=5 |date=September 28, 1916 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163370/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk_ii/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> then early November she gave a talk "Spiritual Internationalism" for the Chicago Peace Society, in cooperation with the Chicago Branch of the Woman's Peace Party,<ref>{{cite journal |title=Among the peace organizations |journal=The Advocate of Peace |volume=78 |issue=11 |pages=342–344 |publisher=World Affairs Institute |date=December 1916 |jstor=20667669 <!--|access-date=May 21, 2015-->}}</ref> then a series on the Baháʼí Faith's view various subjects in nearby Rockford, IL,<ref>* {{cite news|title =Speaks here Monday on "New Economics" |newspaper =Rockford Republic |location =Rockford, IL|page=2 |date=November 11, 1916 }} * {{cite news |title =Spiritual healing Mrs. Ford's subject |newspaper =Rockford Republic |location =Rockford, IL|page=2 |date =November 13, 1916 }} * {{cite news |title =In Society; Announcement is made of two more ...|newspaper =Morning Star |location =Rockford, IL|page =5 |date =November 14, 1916 }}</ref> and then in [[Wilmette, Illinois]] a little over a week later came an announcement of the purchase of increased land area for the [[Baháʼí House of Worship (Wilmette, Illinois)|Baháʼí House of Worship in Wilmette]].<ref>{{cite news |title=$1,000,000 Bahai Temple |newspaper=Riverdale Pointer |location=Dolton, IL|page=7 |date=November 17, 1916 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/riverdale/riverdale-pointer/1916/11-17/page-7 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She returned to Rockford in December for a talk on color.<ref>{{cite news |title =The management of the Ashton Dry Goods firm ...|newspaper =Rockford Republic |location =Rockford, IL|page=4 |date =December 19, 1916 }}</ref>
In January 1917 Ford was back in Washington, D.C.,<ref>*{{cite news |title=Address to Bahai Assembly |newspaper=Washington Post |location=Washington, DC|page=38 |date=January 21, 1917|url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163376/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talks_at/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and in March she was in Boston speaking before the "Free Religious Association of America",<ref>{{cite news |title=Free Religious Association of America |newspaper=Boston Post |location=Boston, MA|page=11 |date=March 3, 1917 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/massachusetts/boston/boston-post/1917/03-03/page-11 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and contributed an article to ''Star of the West'' on "The economic teaching of Abdul-Baha",<ref>{{cite news |title=The economic teaching of Abdul-Baha |author=Mary Hanford Ford |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=8 |issue=1 |pages=3–7; 11–16 |date=March 21, 1917 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=08&page=3 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> which was later included in the first [[Baháʼí literature#Periodicals|Baháʼí World]] volume.<ref name=BWindex/> In April she was in New York at a ''Friends of Abdul Baha'' meeting at [[St. Mark's in-the-Bouwerie]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Friends of Abdul Baha |newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=20 |date=April 14, 1917 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%207/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201917%20Apr%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201917%20Apr%20Grayscale%20%28418%29.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and at the national Baháʼí convention held in Boston,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mashrak-el-Azkar Convention and Bahai Congress |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=8 |issue=9 |page=105 |date=August 20, 1917 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=08&page=105 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and elsewhere in May.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=5 |date=May 26, 1917 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201917%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201917%20Grayscale%20-%204603.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> By August she was in Topeka Kansas giving her first talk in the region presenting the religion.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Ford's Talk |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS|page=6 |date=August 4, 1917 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/543001/talk_by_bahai_mary_hanford_ford/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She mentioned [[H. G. Wells]]' publication, ''[[God the Invisible King]]'', which mentioned the religion (and to Ford's reading matched the idea of [[God in the Baháʼí Faith]]).
By January 1918 Ford was back in the Oakland California area helping to host non-denominational meetings on uplifting people's lives.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Noonday meetings |newspaper=Oakland Tribune |location=Oakland, CA|page=5 |date=January 19, 1918 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/oakland/oakland-tribune/1918/01-19/page-5 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Gives lecture |newspaper=Oakland Tribune |location=Oakland, CA|page=6 |date=March 20, 1918 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/oakland/oakland-tribune/1918/03-20/page-6 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> By April, Ford was also giving a talk on art.<ref>{{cite news |title=What is doing to-morrow |newspaper=Oakland Tribune |location=Oakland, CA|date=April 6, 1918 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/oakland/oakland-tribune/1918/04-06/page-9 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Realm of Music |newspaper=Oakland Tribune |location=Oakland, CA|page=33 |date=April 7, 1918 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/oakland/oakland-tribune/1918/04-07/page-33 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> That December, she was back in Kansas for a weekly series of talks,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Gertrude Canconnon to open music school |newspaper=The Sun |location=Pittsburg, KS|page=5 |date=December 29, 1918 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166402/ford_mh__1918_dec_29__pittsburg/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give the last in a series ...|newspaper=Kansas City Times |location=Kansas City, KS|page=22 |date=January 10, 1919 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/kansas-city/kansas-city-times/1919/01-10/page-22 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as individual talks.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give ...|newspaper=Kansas City Times |location=Kansas City, KS|page=27 |date=February 11, 1919 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/kansas-city/kansas-city-times/1919/02-11/page-27 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Talks to art guild |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS|page=2 |date=February 23, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166366/ford_mh__1919_feb_23_topeka__i/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=(continued) |newspaper=The Topeka Daily Capital |location=Topeka, KS|page=2 |date=February 23, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2166378/ford_mh__1919_feb_23__topeka__ii/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> On January 26, 1919, Ford received another letter/tablet from ʻAbdu'l-Bahá in response to a letter from her.<ref name=DLH/>
In May she was on the program for the national Baháʼí observance during Ridván again,<ref>* {{cite news |title=(program - Reception and Feast of El-Rizwan - Tablet - Woman's Station in this Great Day, read by Mary Hanford Ford,) The Convention of Abdul-Baha |author=Joseph H. Hannen |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=10 |issue=4 |page=(see p. 55) |date=May 17, 1919 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=10&page=55 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Opening of the Convention and Congress - the Feast of El-Rizwan |author=Louis Gregory |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=10 |issue=4 |page=(see pp. 56–58) |date=May 17, 1919 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=10&page=56 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and then addressed the national convention of Baháʼís and her talk published in the ''Star of the West''.<ref>{{cite news |title=To Live the Life |author=Mary Hanford Ford |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=10 |issue=9 |pages=182–183; 186–189 |date=August 20, 1919 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=10&page=182 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
{{Location map+|United States|width=300|float=right|caption=Second tour of talks as a Baháʼí|places= {{Location map~|United States|label=[[Buffalo, New York|Buffalo]]|position=right|lat=42.9047|long=-78.8494}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Sandusky, Ohio|Sandusky]]|position=right|lat=41.4467 |long=-82.7092}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Kansas City, Missouri|Kansas City]]|position=right|lat=39.0997|long=-94.5783}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Pittsburg, Kansas|Pittsburg]]|position=bottom|lat=37.4103|long=-94.6997}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Urbana, Illinois|Urbana]]|position=right|lat=40.1097 |long=-88.2042}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Chicago, Illinois|Chicago]]|position=top|lat=41.8369 |long=-87.6847}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[St. Joseph, Kansas|St. Joseph]]|position=left|lat=39.5097 |long=-97.4061}} {{location map~|United States|label=[[Sterling, Kansas|Sterling]]|position=left|lat=38.2106 |long=-98.2044}} }}
In July she was in [[Buffalo, New York]] giving a talk on the religion,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford of New York ... |newspaper=Buffalo Evening News |location=Buffalo, NY|page=9 |date=July 28, 1919 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%2021/Buffalo%20NY%20Evening%20News/Buffalo%20NY%20Evening%20News%201919/Buffalo%20NY%20Evening%20News%201919%20-%208704.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> then [[Sandusky, Ohio]] in August,<ref>{{cite news |title=Local Bahaists hear address by noted exponent |newspaper=The Sandusky Register |location=Sandusky, OH|page=7 |date=August 12, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2060241/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> [[Pittsburg, Kansas]] in September,<ref>{{cite news |title=Extension study club |newspaper=The Sun |location=Pittsburg, KS|page=6 |date=September 20, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2165840/ford_mh__1919_sept_20_pittsburg/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> [[Urbana, Illinois]] in October,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs Ford to speak on teaching of Abdul {{sic|Boha|nolink=y}} |newspaper=Daily Illini |location=Urbana, IL|page=7 |date=October 11, 1919 |url=http://idnc.library.illinois.edu/cgi-bin/illinois?a=d&d=DIL19191011.2.76 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Chicago, Illinois the next day,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai SS. John Hall ...|newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL|page=10 |date=October 12, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2173126/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk_on/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> before doing a round of locations in Kansas: after a short say in Kansas City she went to [[St. Joseph, Kansas]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford left yesterday ...|newspaper=Kansas City Times |location=Kansas City, KS|page=18 |date=November 7, 1919 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/kansas-city/kansas-city-times/1919/11-07/page-18 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and was scheduled for [[Hutchinson, Kansas]] in January 1920.<ref>* {{cite news |title=A most interesting meeting ...|newspaper=Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=11 |date=December 19, 1919 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/hutchinson/hutchinson-news/1919/12-19/page-11 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Will gives lectures - Mrs. Mary H. Ford will give these next month |newspaper=The Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=11 |date=December 31, 1919 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2173142/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk_series/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> These do indeed occur,<ref>* {{cite news |title=The Apollo Club will not meet ...|newspaper=Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=11 |date=January 3, 1920 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/hutchinson/hutchinson-news/1920/01-03/page-11 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Interested in Russia? |newspaper=Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=5 |date=January 8, 1920 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/hutchinson/hutchinson-news/1920/01-08/page-5 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as a side trip to [[Sterling, Kansas]] for a talk.<ref>{{cite news |title=Lectured at Sterling |newspaper=Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=15 |date=January 8, 1920 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/hutchinson/hutchinson-news/1920/01-08/page-15 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Then Ford was in Hutchinson, Kansas for a reception<ref>{{cite news |title=Ten years ago in 1920 |newspaper=Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=4 |date=January 11, 1930 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/hutchinson/hutchinson-news/1930/01-11/page-4 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> following a week of talks.<ref>{{cite news |title=Famous artist speaks Friday |newspaper=The Hutchinson News |location=Hutchinson, KS|page=8 |date=January 14, 1930 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2169405/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_comments_on/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> But soon she was back in New York and this time for an extended stay.
==Postwar years in New York== In early February 1920, Ford was in New York for a national meeting of Baháʼís.<ref>{{cite news |title=Minutes of the Eleventh Annual Mashrekol-Azkar Convention (continued) |author=Harlan Foster Ober |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=10 |issue=18 |page=(see p. 334) |date=February 7, 1920 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=10&page=334 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She was still around the area a few weeks later in Brooklyn.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai (sic) to sponsor Interracial Dinner in New York City|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=February 21, 1920 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201920%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201920%20Grayscale%20-%201689.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> This was also the time when she met with the First Emmanuel Church community co-presenting on a recent pilgrimage before the Christian community.<ref name=nya200228>{{cite news | title = The Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = February 28, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/33112875/bahais-at-emmanuel-mary-hanford-ford/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> While sporadic at first, she would soon be well ensconced in activities at the Church. First Emmanuel had been founded by 1914 by African American Rev. Richard Manuel Bolden<ref name=Hollinger2023>{{cite book | last = Hollinger | first = Richard | title = The Baha'i Faith and African American Studies |editor1=Loni Bramson |editor2=Layli Maparyan |chapter=The Spread of the Baha'i Faith among African Americans, 1898-1925 | publisher = Lexington Books | date = 2023 | location = Lanham, MY | isbn = 9781666900163 | oclc = 1348935234}}</ref>{{rp|p58}} and his wife Medora.<ref name=Hollinger2023/>{{rp|p278}} They had founded the church in what at the time was the white side of Harlem<ref>* {{cite magazine | title = The Legendary Cotton Club In Harlem 1923 To 1935 | magazine = Harlem World Magazine | date = November 16, 2020 | url =https://www.harlemworldmagazine.com/harlem-history-the-cotton-club/ | access-date = December 9, 2023}} * {{cite news | title = New Negro Church in Harlem | newspaper = Real Estate Record and Builders Guide | page = 641 | date = April 11, 1914 | url =https://rerecord.library.columbia.edu/pdf_files/ldpd_7031148_053_41.pdf | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> after separating from the [[Mother AME Zion Church]]. Within a decade, the black side of Harlem expanded from the Great Migration to re-surround the Boldens' initiative.<ref>{{cite web | title = The Harlem Renaissance: The Development of a New African-American Consciousness |author= Angela Wiley | url = https://academic.kellogg.edu/marklinl/AmLit2/Readings/HarlemRenaissance2.htm | via = Kellogg Community College | date = | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Aside from a personal attachment to Rev. Bolden, the church community was engaged as part of Bolden's vision of the "God with us" even from inside of us.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = March 12, 1914 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/116162602/first-emmanuel-church-later-bahai/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> A number of Bahá'ís responded to this necessarily interracial initiative of a black church in white Harlem that was First Emmanuel before 1918,<ref name=Hollinger2023/>{{rp|p58}} and this expanded such that by January 1920 regular Bahá'í meetings began to be held, and doubled to two different meetings a week, and a second African American minister - James T. Simpson, a chaplain in the U.S.Army during the war joining the Church by January 1920 - being closely involved.<ref name=Hollinger2023/>{{rp|pp58–59}} This is what Ford stepped into in February. Meanwhile, Ford was locally visible in mid-April at St. Mark's-in-the-Bouwerie, another New York church with a close affiliation with Bahá'ís,<ref>{{cite news |title=St. Mark's-in-the-Bouwerie ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=April 24, 1920 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201920%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201920%20Grayscale%20-%203855.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> mixing explicitly Baháʼí oriented talks and music or art topics. In May at the meeting during Ridvan Ford addressed the attendees of the National Bahá'í convention in New York.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=Twelfth Annual Mashrekol-Azkar Convention and Bahai Congress |author=Louis Gregory |magazine=Star of the West |volume=11 |issue=4 |page=(see p. 63) |date=May 17, 1920 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=11&page=63 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
===First Emmanuel Church and ''Reality'' magazine===
From late July 1920 through October 1922 Ford became a fixture at First Emmanuel Church in a variety of settings. Back in February she had co-presented to the church community<ref name=nya200228/> but in late July<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = July 31, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114241226/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> she was part of the Thursday evening "Rainbow Circle" meetings that had been founded by Bahá'ís and then moved to First Emmanuel.<ref name=Hollinger2023/>{{rp|p59}} A second set of Bahá'í meetings also took form, the "Baha'i Association", by March.<ref>{{cite news | title = Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = March 13, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115150648/bahai-association-two-meetings/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Most of the coverage of these is from the African American newspaper, the ''[[New York Age]]''.
By early August, a number of Bahá'ís, including Ford, gathered at the Circle meeting, including a Persian dinner and presentations of many, including Mabel Rice-Wray of Detroit, (mother of [[Edris Rice-Wray Carson]],) and Rev. [[Howard Colby Ives]], (later author of ''Portals of Freedom'').<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = August 7, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115151340/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> The group formed its own organizational constitution,<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = August 14, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115151570/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and Ford continued to be a regular into September.<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = August 21, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/21797713/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church-i/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = August 28, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/21797796/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = September 4, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115151914/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Across the same period<ref>{{cite magazine |title=Mary Hanford Ford (letter) |magazine=Star of the West |volume=11 |issue=9 |pages=149–150 |date=August 20, 1920 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=11&page=149 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> she was the main presenter during an extended exhibition with a model of the Bahá'í Temple during its stay in New York.<ref name=NY>{{cite web |author=Hussein Ahdieh |title=Highlights of the first 40 years of the Baháʼí Faith in New York, City of the Covenant - 1892–1932 |publisher=Local Spiritual Assembly of the Baháʼís of New York City |date=January 29, 2012 |url=http://www.abdulbahainnewyork.org/pdf/first40/NY_Bahai_First_40.pdf |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref><ref name=remmeber>{{cite news |title=The Mashriqu'l-Adkhár in America |author=Shahnaz Waite |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=21 |issue=1 |pages=7–17 |date=April 1930 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=21&page=9 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> By late September Bolden had received a letter from 'Abdu'l-Bahá apparently accepting his declaration as a Bahá'í,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = September 25, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114245190/bahais-meet-at-first-emmanuel-van/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> because he and his church we all proclaimed as Bahá'ís in the DC ''Washington Bee'' mid-month.<ref>{{cite news | title = Growing interest in Bahai Movement (sic) | newspaper = Washington Bee | location = Washington, DC | page = 7 | date = September 18, 1920 | url =https://www.genealogybank.com/newspaper-clippings/bahais-louis-gregory-fazel-mazandarani-william-randal-rev-richard-bolden.../mnmpuoubuzbntwydznntyunvaprdpoep_wma-gateway016_1672518964106 |via=GenealogyBank.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> In September she also spoke at another Bahá'í center of activity, the ''Omar Khayyam'', opened by some Persian Baháʼís.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=A new center...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=10 |page=51 |date=October 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=501 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> Then Ford published an article "The Rainbow Circle" in ''Reality'' magazine published in the October issue describing some of the history and names involved in this project that came to First Emmanuel,<ref>{{cite magazine |title=The Rainbow Circle |author=Mary Hanford Ford|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=2 |issue=10 |pages=31–34 |date=October 1920|page= |url= |access-date=}}</ref> and Bahá'í meetings at the Church became regular entries in the magazine as well.<ref>* {{cite journal |title=The Rainbow Circle|author=Mary Hanford Ford|journal=Reality |volume=2 |issue=10 |pages=31–34 |date= Oct 1920 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=The Rainbow Circle|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=2 |issue=11 |page=40 |date=Nov 1920 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=The Rainbow Circle|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|author=Mary Hanford Ford|magazine=Reality |volume=2 |issue= 12|pages=37–39 |date=Dec 1920 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=The Rainbow Circle|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume= 3|issue=1 |page=40 |date=Jan 1921 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=The Rainbow Circle|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=4 |pages=40–41 |date=Apr 1921 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title= An unusual and unique...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=6 |page=25 |date=Aug 1921 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai (sic) Activities; The Rainbow Circle...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=10 |page=50 |date= Oct 1921|url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; The Rainbow Circle...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=11 |page=52 |date=Nov 1921 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; The Rainbow Circle...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=5 |issue=1 |page= 48|date=Jan 1922 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; The Rainbow Circle...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=5 |issue= 5|pages=52–53 |date=May 1922 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; The Rainbow Circle...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=5 |issue=6 |page=47 |date=Jun 1922 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; The 'Knights and Ladies of the Round Table'...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume= 5|issue= 8|page=48 |date=Aug 1922 |url= |access-date=}} * {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; With the fall and winter seasons...|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=5 |issue=11 |page=59 |date=Nov 1922 |url= |access-date=}}</ref> Ford presented at the early October Sunday morning services, while at the evening service Bolden presented Bahá'í Walter Guy a letter of introduction to the churches in St. Augustine, Florida.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = October 9, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/10318609/first-emmanuel-meeting-of-rainbow/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Rev. Simpson now contributed his own talk at the Circle meeting entitled "How I became a Bahai(sic)".<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = October 30, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/13386737/bahai-meetings-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Ford made her regular appearance at the Circle in early November,<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 3 | date = November 6, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114246611/bahai-meetings-at-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> gave a talk on Luke 14 at the evening church service in the middle of the month,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = November 20, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/13386214/bahai-meeting-led-by-m-van-blacum/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and then gave a talk "Holy Communion and its meaning" as the Sunday morning sermon about the early history of Christian meetings, to close the month.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = November 27, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/13386514/bahai-meetings-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> "Baha'i Association" meetings were named in coverage from November, and Bolden led the ''Circle'' meeting that included readings from 'Abdu'l-Bahá.<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = November 13, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115153538/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = November 27, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/13386514/bahai-meetings-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Across Thanksgiving and into early December Ford attended multiple meetings at the Church,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of Greater New York; Manhattan and the Bronx; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = December 4, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114247439/bahais-meeting-at-emmanuel-mr-zimms/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and then gave a sermon on Isaiah, Zachariah, and Revelations and then Bolden, [[Urbain Ledoux]] and Ford were among those that took part in the Circle meeting.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = December 11, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115153619/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Before Christmas, Bolden had founded, and had a performance of, an inter-racial choir in New York as a direct consequence of the Bahá'í ''Rainbow Circle'',<ref>{{cite news | title = Immanuel Choir in unique intra-racial program | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = December 18, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/article/the-new-york-age-bahais-in-first-emmanu/114247542/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and contributed an article "America's Place in the World" published in ''Reality'' December 1920.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=America's Place In the World |author=Rev. Ricard Manuel Bolden|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=2 |issue=12 |pages=13–15 |date=December 1920|url= |access-date=}}</ref> Across Christmas week both the Baha'i Association and the Rainbow Circle had meetings including Ford and Bolden.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = December 25, 1920 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286399/bahai-faith-mentions-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref>
Ford opened January 1921, introducing returned Bahá'í pilgrims Mrs. Cooper and Mrs. Franklin of California at the Sunday evening service.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | pages = 1 [https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115154047/bahai-associated-first-emmanuel/ 7] | date = January 1, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115154030/bahai-associated-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Bolden and Ford attended the mid-week usual Circle meeting and then Ford read from Abdu'l-Bahá's comments on the Trinity at the close of the morning Sunday service.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = January 8, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286403/mentions-of-bahai-faith-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Bolden also took up writing another article for ''Reality'' magazine, "Our Future Government" in the January 1921 edition.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=Our Future Government |author=Ricard Manuel Bolden|editor1=Wandeyne Deuth |editor2= E.J. Deuth|magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=1 |pages= 20–21|date=January 1921|url=https://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991&view=1up&seq=28|access-date=December 7, 2023}}</ref> Mid-January included the Association and Circle meetings and this time there were letters from other cities, including Bahá'ís [[Harriet Gibbs Marshall]] of DC, [[Sadie and Mabry Oglesby|Mabry Oglesby]] of Boston, and others.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = January 15, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114806400/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Association and Circle meetings continued,<ref>* {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 5 | date = January 22, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115154308/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = January 29, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/21797877/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church-i/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = February 12, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/71944558/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church-mary/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and then, in addition to those, [[Urbain Ledoux]] contributed to the Sunday morning sermon,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = February 5, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286405/bahai-faith-mentioned-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and Ford addressed the children's Sunday School and the afternoon Baha'i meeting while Rev. James Simpson presented on the Baha'i Revelation and talked about the prospect of his pioneering.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = February 19, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114808383/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> By March, Bolden was a consulting editor for ''Reality'' magazine along with Ford, Albert Vall, Howard MacNutt, and Horace Holley which continued for a time.<ref>{{cite journal |title=Reality |journal=Reality |volume=3|issue=3 |page=3 |date=March 1921 |url=https://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991&view=1up&seq=103 |access-date=December 8, 2023}}</ref> Again there were regular Association and Circle meetings,<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7M | date = April 23, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115148881/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = April 30, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115148952/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> In addition to printing articles of hers, ''Reality'' also noted some of her activities. She gave a talk "Tribute to Lathrop Ripley" (probably for the artist associated with the Rocky Mountain National Park region),<ref>{{cite book |author=Charles Edwin Hewes |title=Songs of the Rockies |url=https://archive.org/details/songsrockies00hewegoog |year=1922 |publisher=Egerton-Palmer Press |pages=[https://archive.org/details/songsrockies00hewegoog/page/n219 197]–198}}</ref> March 6, 1921, in Kansas City, Missouri, her last known talk in that region, at the age of 64.<ref>{{cite news |author=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Tribute to Lathrop Ripley |volume=3 |issue=13 |newspaper=Estes Park Trail |location=Estes Park, CO|date=November 23, 1923 |url=http://eparkhives.com/pdf/1923-EPTrail-P2.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Then April 13 she was at a meeting at the New York "Bahai Library" center at 416 Madison Ave. where she gave talks along with [[James Ferdinand Morton Jr.|James F. Morton Jr.]] and [[Juliet Thompson]].<ref name=MayReality>{{cite magazine |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford ...|magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=5 |page=43 |date=May 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=235 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> The April 1921 issue of ''Reality'' noted she went to Florida to be treated by Walter A. Guy in St. Augustine, Florida.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford ...|magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=4 |page=43 |date=April 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=189 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> The issue that came out in May noted her return and "constant daily attendance the Baha'i Library",<ref name=MayReality/> and she was back to speak to the children's Sunday School of First Emmanuel in early May,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 10 | date = May 7, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/20571935/bahais-meet-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> the Mother's Day Sunday morning service,<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = May 14, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286415/bahai-faith-mentioned-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and at the Sunday morning sermon late in May.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = May 28, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115156236/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> [[Louis Gregory]] came to give a talk as well as news of the race amity meeting of the Bahá'ís in Washington DC.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = June 18, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114860562/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Ford again spoke to the Sunday School children,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = June 25, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115149576/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> as meetings and participations continued,<ref>* {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = June 11, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/20571864/bahai-and-rainbow-circle-meetings-at/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = July 2, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115149607/bahais-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> when in July Ford was named as president of the Baha'i Association.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = July 9, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/33116764/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-mary-hanford/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> In mid-July Ford spoke to the Sunday School children again and presided at the Baha'i Association, in addition to speaking at the Circle meeting.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 3 | date = July 16, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114906477/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Late in July Bolden and Ford addressed the Sunday School with a "very large attendance... a number of visitors present" together, while the Baha'i Association and friends "motored" to the Teaneck Baha'i Center at West Englewood, the "Roy Wilhelm camp".<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = July 30, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115156657/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Regular meetings continued including occasional readings from ''[[The Hidden Words]]'' and ''[[Some Answered Questions]]'' and the occasional visitor,<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = August 6, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286419/bahai-faith-mentioned-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = August 20, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114906759/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 3, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115149678/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 10, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286421/bahai-faith-mentioned-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 17, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/4753416/bahai-mary-hanford-ford-meeting-with/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 24, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114907103/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = October 8, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115149879/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = October 15, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114907382/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = October 22, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/4753603/bahai-meeting-with-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = November 12, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114909175/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and Ford again gave the Sunday morning sermon in late October.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = October 29, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115157475/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> The second week of November Bolden's sermon pointed to the influence of dwellers on God's Holy mountain on peace plans of the time, though he did not elaborate on what he meant.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = November 19, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/21797226/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Ford was also praised for "her faithfulness to the church" at the end of November,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = November 26, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115157749/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and the next week the news was shared of the death of 'Abdu'l-Bahá.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = December 10, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/19560104/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> The second week of December Ford gave the Sunday morning sermon on "What is meant by blasphemy against the Holy Ghost?".<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = December 17, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115186547/bahais-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> {{Location map+|United States Manhattan|caption=Baha'i talks in New York City||default_size=100|places= {{Location map~|United States Manhattan|lat=40.7567|long= -73.9764|label =Bahai Library(1)|position =bottom}} {{Location map~|United States Manhattan|lat=40.8005|long= -73.9515|label =First Emmanuel Church|position =top}} }} It was in this time that orientalist [[E. G. Browne]] made a eulogy observation of the effect of 'Abdu'l-Bahá that "One of the most notable practical results of the Baha'i ethical teaching in the United States has been, according to the recent testimony of an impartial and qualified observer, the establishment in Baha'i circles in New York of a real fraternity between black and white, and an unprecedented lifting of the 'colour bar', described by the said observer as 'almost miraculous'."<ref>{{cite journal |author=E. G. Browne|author-link =E. G. Browne |title =Sir ʻAbdu'l-Baha 'Abbas: Died 28th November, 1921 |journal =The Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland |issue =1 |pages =145–146 |publisher =Cambridge University Press |date =January 1922 |jstor =25209873}}</ref>
Through the winter and into the spring of 1922 regular meetings and readings continued at the Church,<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | pages = 1, [https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115186601/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-ii/ 2] | date = December 31, 1921 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115186579/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-i/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = January 21, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114913226/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | page = 7 | location = New York, NY | date = February 4, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/4753486/mrs-copeland-presided-at-bahai/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = February 11, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5770559/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = February 18, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/268918/several-meeting-with-bahais-more-than/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = February 25, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115162054/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of Greater New York; Manhattan and the Bronx; Rev. R. M. Bolden's birthday celebrated | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = March 11, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115162711/bahai-richard-m-bolden-birthday-events/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 10 | date = March 11, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5770865/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> amidst which a letter from Haifa on the funeral-memorial of 'Abdu'l-Bahá was read.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = January 7, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/286428/bahai-faith-mentioned-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> In addition, in March Ford and Bolden again cooperated on the Sunday morning service,<ref>{{cite news | title = News of the Churches, First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = March 4, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115162210/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and Ford contributed on behalf of the Rainbow Circle on the occasion of Bolden's birthday.<ref>{{cite news | title = News of Greater New York; Manhattan and the Bronx; Rev. R. M. Bolden's birthday celebrated | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = March 11, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115162711/bahai-richard-m-bolden-birthday-events/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> In March Baháʼís sponsored a dinner in New York City as an instance of [[Baháʼí Faith and the unity of humanity|its teaching on race unity]] – Ford spoke at the event along with [[Louis G. Gregory]], [[Horace Holley (Baháʼí)|Horace Holley]], [[W. E. B. DuBois]] and others.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai to sponsor interracial dinner in New York City |newspaper=The Pittsburgh Courier |location=Pittsburgh, PA|page=2 |date=March 12, 1922 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%2023/Pittsburgh%20PA%20Courier/Pittsburgh%20PA%20Courier%201932/Pittsburgh%20PA%20Courier%201932%20-%200191.pdf |via=FultonHistory.com |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Meanwhile at the First Emmanuel Church, regular Bahá'í meetings, readings, talks of visitors, etc, even talks by other visiting pastors, continued through the summer of 1922.<ref>* {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 1, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5770928/bahai-association-meeting-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 8, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/114957870/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper =The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 15, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5771525/bahai-meeting-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, New York | page = 2 | date = April 22, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5771768/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 29, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115163417/bahai-rev-richard-bolden-in/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 22, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5771768/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = April 29, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115163417/bahai-rev-richard-bolden-in/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = May 6, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5772036/bahai-meetings-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 2 | date = May 13, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/4814277/bahai-meeting-at-first-emmanuel-with/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = May 27, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/21798070/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = June 10, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115163724/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = July 8, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115164159/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = August 5, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115164214/bahais-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = August 5, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115164214/bahais-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 2, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115165088/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref>
The break in the pattern was in September 1922. The first sign of a problem was when Revs. Bolden and Simpson were announced as political adversaries.<ref>{{cite news | title = Seek to oust Fallace | newspaper = New York Herald | location = New York, NY | page = 18 | date = September 17, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/article/new-york-herald-bahais-james-t-simpson/116445417/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> The atmosphere was surely highly charged with bullets at at least one partisan meeting.<ref>{{cite news | title = Bullets fly in New York's YWCA rooms | newspaper = The Chicago Whip | location = Chicago, IL | page = 1 | date = September 23, 1922 | url =https://chroniclingamerica.loc.gov/lccn/sn86056950/1922-09-23/ed-1/seq-1/ |via=ChroniclingAmerica.loc.gov | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> This was before [[Shoghi Effendi]] standardized avoiding political party affiliations in the religion, a principle started by 'Abdu'l-Bahá in America when asked.<ref>{{cite book | last =Dahl | first =Roger M. | title =Community Histories |editor1= Richard Hollinger |chapter=History of the Kenosha Bahá'í Community 1897-1980 | publisher =Kalimát Press | series =Studies in Bahá'í Histories | volume =6 |editor2=series ed Anthony A. Lee |pages=33–4 | date =1992 | location =Los Angeles | isbn =9780933770768 |oclc= 32305770}}</ref> And this was right after the death of Medora Bolden, the pastor's wife.<ref>{{cite news | title = Rev. Bolden's wife died September 18 | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = September 23, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/article/the-new-york-age-obit-of-bahai-medora-b/115165560/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> Among the outpouring of sympathy, flowers were sent including from the "Baha'i Headquarters" and the Rainbow Circle, but all that Simpson could do was send a condolence letter.<ref>{{cite news | title = Funeral services for Mrs. Medora E. Bolden | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = September 30, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/68988348/1922-livingstone-ny-age-9-30-1922/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> We don't know why this situation degenerated, but following this, meetings became intermittent and then ended in October,<ref>* {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 9, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115165226/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = September 16, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115165481/anniversary-of-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 7 | date = September 23, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115165681/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = October 7, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/268949/bahai-meeting-among-listed-at-first/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = October 14, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115165813/bahais-at-first-emmanuel-church/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite news | title = News of the Churches; First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 8 | date = October 21, 1922 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/5773046/bahais-met-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> though still mentioned in ''Reality'' in November.<ref>* {{cite magazine |title=Bahai(sic) Activities; With the fall and winter seasons...|magazine=Reality |volume=5 |issue=11 |page=59 |date=Nov 1922 |url= |access-date=}}</ref> Rev. Simpson has yet to be found at further Bahá'í meetings, while Bolden appeared at the Bahá'í meetings across 1926-7,<ref>* {{cite news | title = Followers of Baha meet in Teaneck in annual session | newspaper = The Record | location = Hackensack, New Jersey | page = 13 [https://www.newspapers.com/clip/27977359/profile-of-annual-teaneck-interracial/ continued] | date = June 28, 1926 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/27977346/profile-of-annual-teaneck-interracial/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }} * {{cite magazine | title = Souvenir Feast of 'Abdu'l-Bahá |author= Keith Ransom-Kehler | magazine = Star of the West |volume=18 |number=1 | page = 147 | date = Apr 1927 | url =https://bahai.works/index.php?title=File:SW_Volume18.pdf&page=147 | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref> and Louis Gregory returned to speak at First Emmanuel in the same period.<ref>{{cite news | title = First Emmanuel Church | newspaper = The New York Age | location = New York, NY | page = 16 | date = December 18, 1926 | url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/115006039/bahai-louis-gregory-at-first-emmanuel/ |via=Newspapers.com | access-date =December 9, 2023 }}</ref>
Meanwhile, in addition to Ford's regular work through the First Emmanuel Church and after her initial writing for ''Reality'' she began to contribute to the magazine more systematically through 1921: * January – "[[Claude Bragdon]] on the Fourth Dimension"<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Claude Bragdon on the Fourth Dimension |magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=1 |pages=23–24, 29–31 |date=January 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=30 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * February – "The Great Memorial of [[George Grey Barnard]]"<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Great Memorial of George Grey Barnard |magazine=Reality |volume=3 |issue=2 |pages=35–39 |date=February 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=89 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * June – "The Current Art" (reviewing the recent work of [[George Luks]], [[Victor Higgins]], and others as well as general exhibits)<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=7 |pages=20–23 |date=June 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=262 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * July – "The Symbolism of the [[Baháʼí House of Worship (Wilmette, Illinois)|Baha'i Temple]]"<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Symbolism of the Temple |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=7 |pages=14–18 |date=July 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=306 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> (note "The Current Art" from June was reprinted in July)<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=7 |issue=4 |pages=29–31 |date=July 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=607 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * August – "The Current Art" as a recurring column appears reviewing recent sales at the [[Anderson Galleries]] as a kick off to review American Art more broadly and the notes Marco Zim had done a portrait of ʻAbdu'l-Bahá and hoped to do more.<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=8 |pages=29–31 |date=August 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=359 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * September – "The Current Art", in which she examines some cases of why artists are artists and then noted another portrait of ʻAbdu'l-Bahá - that of Frances Soule Campbell<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=9 |pages=27–29 |date=September 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=417 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> who had been a published artist of some years.<ref>{{cite news |author=Eleanro R. Ingraham |title=Portraits of well known people |newspaper=San Francisco Call |location=San Francisco, CA|page=6 |date=October 3, 1909 |url=http://cdnc.ucr.edu/cgi-bin/cdnc?a=d&d=SFC19091003.2.181.7 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> * October – "The Current Art", noted and defended the criticized showing of Impressionist and Post-impressionist exhibit at the Metropolitan Museum that had affected American artists and then noted a bust of Dante by [[Paolo Abbate]] and work by Louis Keila.<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=10 |pages=33–36 |date=October 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=483 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * November – "The Current Art" notes the struggle of the wealthy with prospects of managing wealth with art as a need of the soul sometimes lost in the materialism of wealth and goes on to review notes [[Charles W. Bartlett]] and returns to the work of Marco Zim who has background as sculptor, painter, and etcher.<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=11 |pages=33–36 |date=November 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=541 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> * December – "The Current Art" noted a traveling exhibit of American art showing in New York noting many artists and the inability to cover it all in just one article.<ref>{{cite magazine |author=Mary Hanford Ford |author-link=Mary Hanford Ford |title=The Current Art |magazine=Reality |volume=4 |issue=12 |pages=41–44 |date=December 1921 |url=http://babel.hathitrust.org/cgi/pt?id=nyp.33433081895991;view=1up;seq=607 |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref>
From April to September 1922 Ford took on general editor responsibilities of ''Reality'' magazine.<ref name=Smith1984>{{cite journal |author=Peter Smith |author-link= Peter Smith (historian)|title=Reality Magazine: Editorship and Ownership of an American Baháʼí Periodical |journal=From Iran East and West: Studies in Babi and Baháʼí History |volume=2 |pages=135–156 |date=1984 |url=http://bahai-library.com/smith_reality_magazine |access-date=May 4, 2015}}</ref> Ford tried to navigate between positions she saw as "illiberalism" in the religion<ref name=Smith1984/> on the one side and editorial directives by then owner of the magazine - Herold Sweetser Robinson - that Ford failed to sustain when Robinson took the magazine down an editorial path unsupported by the mainstream in the religion.<ref name=Smith1984/> See [[Baháʼí divisions#Shoghi Effendi as Guardian|Divisions over Shoghi Effendi as Guardian]].
===New York and United Kingdom and Italy=== Ford was remembered as an organizing focus of the Chicago Culture Club in April 1923.<ref>{{cite news |title=Chicago Culture Club |newspaper=Oak Park Oak Leaves |location=Oak Park, IL|page=70 |date=April 14, 1923 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/oak-park/oak-park-oak-leaves/1923/04-14/page-70 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Most years in the 1920s she was known to visit Italy and introduced the religion to later Hand of the Cause [[Ugo Giachery]].<ref name=Italy>{{cite encyclopedia|author=Julio Savi |title =Italy: History of the Baháʼí Faith |encyclopedia =draft for ''The Baha'i Encyclopedia'' |publisher =Bahai-library.com |date =1992 |url =http://bahai-library.com/savi_encyclopedia_italy |access-date = August 5, 2017}}</ref> Perhaps some part of each Summer - Fall would be in Italy.
Ford's first known talks at [[Green Acre]], since joining the religion there 22 years earlier, were in July.<ref>* {{cite news |title=At Green Acre |newspaper=Portsmouth Herald |location=Portsmouth, NH|page=10 |date=July 9, 1923 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-hampshire/portsmouth/portsmouth-herald/1923/07-09/page-10 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=At Green Acre |newspaper=Portsmouth Herald |location=Portsmouth, NH|page=7 |date=July 11, 1923 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-hampshire/portsmouth/portsmouth-herald/1923/07-11/page-7 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In February 1924 Ford gave a talk on "Communication between the Two Worlds" at a Spiritualist meeting,<ref>{{cite news |title=Inspirational Church of divine thought ...|newspaper=The Sun and the Globe |location=New York, NY|page=15 |date=February 16, 1924 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201924/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201924%20-%200431.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as the Bahai Library in August,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai Library ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=9 |date=August 23, 1924 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%207/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201924%20Aug%2016-31%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201924%20Aug%2016-31%20Grayscale%20-%200317.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and November.<ref>{{cite news |title=The force behind the new civilization ...|newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=10 |date=November 15, 1924 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201924%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201924%20Grayscale%20-%207311.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In December she talked for the Woman's Advance Club.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford to lecture before Woman's Advance Club |newspaper=The Daily Review |location=Long Island, NY|page=6 |date=November 26, 1924 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%2023/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review%201924%20Oct-Feb%201925/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review%201924%20Oct-Feb%201925%20-%200426.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Advance Club to hear Miss M. H. Ford |newspaper=The Daily Review |location=Long Island, NY|page=3 |date=December 10, 1924 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%2023/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review%201924%20Oct-Feb%201925/Freeport%20NY%20Daily%20Review%201924%20Oct-Feb%201925%20-%200529.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
Sometime in 1924 Ford encountered Philip Leonard Green who two decades later would recall encountering the religion by contact from Ford and her gift of her book "The World of Abdu'l-Baha".<ref name=Philip>{{cite news |title=May 24 |newspaper=Baháʼí News |pages=17 |date=September 1944 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=02&page=643 |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref>
January 1925 opens with talks at the Bahai Library at 250 W 57th St.,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=7 |date=January 3, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%200050.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=24 |date=January 3, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%200108.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=28 |date=January 24, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%201303.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=23 |date=January 31, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%201676.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as attended a banquet at a Hindu center.<ref>* {{cite news |title=New Hindu Religious Center |newspaper=New York Post |location=New York, NY|page=14 |date=January 5, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201935%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201935%20Grayscale%20-%200161.pdf |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref> Her talks at the Baháʼí Center continue in February,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Worldwide unity of Races and Religions ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=1 |date=February 7, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%201068.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=27 |date=February 7, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%202054.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title="The Divine art of Living"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=9 |date=February 21, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%201461.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=21 |date=February 21, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%202801.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Lecture ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=24 |date=February 28, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale%20-%203152.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> March,<ref>* {{cite news |title="Success and reality"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=9 |date=March 7, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%201839.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title="The new
