In September Ford was remembered for her contribution to the Arché club studies.<ref>{{cite news |title=News of the Chicago Women's Clubs: Arche Club Opens Its 37th Season |newspaper=Chicago Daily Tribune |location=Chicago, IL|page=E8 |date=September 27, 1925 |url= <!--|access-date=April 18, 2015-->}}</ref>
Ford's talks at the Bahai Library pick up in October.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai Library ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=10 |date=October 17, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20a%20Grayscale%20-%204520.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - "The Temple"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=11 |date=October 24, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%207787.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In October Ford also represented the Baháʼís at a multi-faith "tribute" to Judaism in West Virginia.<ref>{{cite news |title=Members of all creeds pay tribute to Judaism |newspaper=Charleston Daily Mail |location=Charleston, West Virginia |page=1 |date=October 27, 1925 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/west-virginia/charleston/charleston-daily-mail/1925/10-27/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Many faiths pay tribute to Judaism |newspaper=Kokomo Tribune |location=Kokomo, IN|page=6 |date=October 27, 1925 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/indiana/kokomo/kokomo-tribune/1925/10-27/page-6 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Many cults met to pay tribute |newspaper=Sedalia Capital |location=Sedalia, Missouri |page=8 |date=October 28, 1925 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/missouri/sedalia/sedalia-capital/1925/10-28/page-8 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Many cults met to pay tribute |newspaper=San Antonio Express |location=San Antonio, TX|page=2 |date=October 28, 1925 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/texas/san-antonio/san-antonio-express/1925/10-28/page-2 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
Ford contributed a brief definition of who Baháʼu'lláh was in ''Hartmann's Who's Who in Occult, Psychic and Spiritual Realms'' published in November,<ref>{{cite book |author=William C. Hartman |title=Hartmann's Who's Who in Occult, Psychic and Spiritual Realms|publisher=The Occult Press |date=November 1925 |page=11 |url=http://www.ehbritten.org/docs/1925_hartmann_whos_who_in_occult_psychic_and_spiritual_realms_r.pdf}}</ref> and then Ford took up talks in the Bahai Library again.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - "What you can do with prayer"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=7 |date=November 7, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%208344.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Library ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=8 |date=November 7, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20a%20Grayscale%20-%205872.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - "The United States of the World"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=9 |date=November 28, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%209119.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She finished out the year still doing talks at the Bahai Library.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai Library ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=16 |date=December 5, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20a%20Grayscale%20-%207639.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=How to be happy always |newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=13 |date=December 12, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20-%209707.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Library ...|newspaper=The New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=18 |date=December 12, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201925%20a%20Grayscale%20-%208118.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title="When will Christmas come?"...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=7 |date=December 26, 1925 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201925%20a%20-%200219.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>

|access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> but there was then a break until December during which the old "Bahai Library" center was closed and a new center was opened at 116 W 49th St.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - New Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=3 |date=December 4, 1926 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163381/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - New Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>{{Location map+|United States Manhattan|caption=early Baha'i centers in New York City|default_width=300|places= {{Location map~|United States {{Location map~|United States Manhattan|lat=40.7647|long= -73.9780|label =Bahai Center(2)|position =right}} }} She finished out the year speaking before the Pan American Commercial Congress which was NY|page=26 |date=December 14, 1926 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspapers%207/New%20York%20NY%20Times/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201926%20Dec%20%20%201-10%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Times%201926%20Dec%20%20Grayscale%20%28297%29.pdf p. 31 |date=December 18, 1926 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201926/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201926%20-%208446.pdf |access-date=April 18, |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2536419/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk_at/ |access-date=June 2, 2015}}</ref>
January 1927 began similarly at the new Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - New Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=January 8, 1927 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163385/bahais_mary_hanford_ford_and_sophie/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and in February,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - New Bahai Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=17 |date=February 12, 1927 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201927/New%20York%20NY%20%201927%20-%200460.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - New Bahai Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=25 |date=February 19, 1927 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201927/New%20York%20NY%20%201927%20-%200690.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} *{{cite news |title=Bahai - New Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=10 |date=February 19, 1927 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201927%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201927%20a%20Grayscale%20-%201880.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> though there was then a gap until April.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - New Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=April 2, 1927 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/1260512/bahai_meeting_mary_hanford_ford/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In May Ford was on radio station WGL (later bought by [[WADO]]) and she was cut off the air while speaking to a banquet audience of the All Nations Association, an international peace organization,<ref>{{cite news |title=All Nations Association presented with statue |newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=9 |date=November 19, 1929 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2384886/all_nations_association_is_a_peace/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> in praise of [[Amelia Gade Corson]] because the manager declared it a pacifist talk, which, according to him, "was not in line for the occasion".<ref>* {{cite news |title=Pacifist talk hushed by radio station WGL: Mrs. Mary H. Ford's address at farewell to Mrs. Corson is cut off the air |newspaper=New York Times |location=New York, NY|page=23 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://query.nytimes.com/gst/abstract.html?res=9B0DEEDB123FE03ABC4B53DFB366838C639EDE |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite journal |title=The Freedom of the Air |journal=Radio Broadcast |volume=12 |issue=2 |page=117 |editor=Willis Kingsley Wing |date=December 1927 |url=https://archive.org/stream/radiobroadcast12gardrich#page/117/mode/1up |access-date=May 10, 2015}}</ref> The news of the action was carried by [[Associated Press]] and the story printed variously in the United States.<ref>{{cite book |author=Douglas B. Craig |title=Fireside Politics: Radio and Political Culture in the United States, 1920-1940 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=haWh203m7aIC&pg=PA114 |date=July 7, 2005 |publisher=JHU Press |isbn=978-0-8018-8312-5 |page=114}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |author=Benjamin, Louise M |title=Freedom of the Air and the Public Interest |url=https://archive.org/details/freedomofairpubl0000benj |url-access=registration |publisher=SIU Press |isbn=978-0-8093-8803-5 |page=[https://archive.org/details/freedomofairpubl0000benj/page/139 139]|year=2001 }}</ref><ref>* {{cite news |title=Radio speaker gets "the air"; Mrs. Mary Ford cut off after launching into pacifist argument |newspaper=Lowell Sun |location=Lowell, OH|page=20 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/massachusetts/lowell/lowell-sun/1927/05-03/page-20 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Pacifist halted at radio station |newspaper=Decatur Review |location=Decatur, IL|page=1 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/illinois/decatur/decatur-review/1927/05-03/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Switches "Mike" on pacifist talk, music drowns out speech |newspaper=Emporia Gazette |location=Emporia, Kansas |page=1 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/kansas/emporia/emporia-gazette/1927/05-03/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Radio speaker gets "the air"; Mrs. Mary Ford cut off after launching into pacifist argument |newspaper=Lowell Sun |location=Lowell, MA|page=4 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/massachusetts/lowell/lowell-sun/1927/05-03/page-4 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Stops pacifist broadcast |newspaper=Portsmouth Herald |location=Portsmouth, NH|page=2 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-hampshire/portsmouth/portsmouth-herald/1927/05-03/page-2 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Broadcast cut off as speech turns pacifist |newspaper=Oakland Tribune |location=Oakland, CA|page=19 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/oakland/oakland-tribune/1927/05-03/page-19 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Broadcast cut off as speech turns pacifist |newspaper=Bakersfield Californian |location=Bakersfield, CA|page=2 |date=May 3, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/california/bakersfield/bakersfield-californian/1927/05-03/page-2 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Stopped pacific speech of Mary H. Ford in NY|newspaper=Jefferson City Tribune Post |location=Jefferson City, Missouri |page=1 |date=May 5, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/missouri/jefferson-city/jefferson-city-tribune-post/1927/05-03/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Radio speech is interrupted |newspaper=The Ogden Standard-Examiner |location=Ogden, UT|page=2 |date=May 4, 1927 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2384605/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_pacificst/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Radio speech is interrupted; Alleged pacifist pleas cut short by station manager |newspaper=Ogden Standard Examiner |location=Ogden, UT|page=2 |date=May 4, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/utah/ogden/ogden-standard-examiner/1927/05-04/page-2 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> It was one of several incidents with that station.<ref>{{cite book |author1=Bill Jaker |author2=Frank Sulek |author3=Peter Kanze |title=The Airwaves of New York: Illustrated Histories of 156 AM Stations in the Metropolitan Area, 1921-1996 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=pLTB4E5wVBAC&pg=PA77 |date=May 1, 1998 |publisher=McFarland |isbn=978-0-7864-3872-3 |page=77}}</ref> Ford later released a statement:
<blockquote>The All Nations association for which i was speaking feels Denmark represents the ideal for which it is working, having created peace through disarmament, I said that as Mrs. Corson, a native of Denmark, had married an American, better feeling would be created between the two countries because of the fame of this woman.<ref>{{cite news |title=Woman's pacifist speech cut off |newspaper=The Spokesman-Review |location=Spokane, Washington |page=12 |date=May 4, 1927 |url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=1314&dat=19270504&id=D-oUAAAAIBAJ&pg=6745,872123&hl=en |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref></blockquote>
Congressman [[Emanuel Celler]] added the incident to his list of censorship of liberal views published in June.<ref>{{cite news |author=Robert Mack |title=Congressman aroused by censorship on the air; cites "Horrible" example |newspaper=Portsmouth Daily Times |location=Portsmouth, OH|page=14 |date=June 8, 1927 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/ohio/portsmouth/portsmouth-daily-times/1927/06-08/page-14 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In September Ford herself was in London and Rome.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Urges Unity; Mary Hanford Ford ...|newspaper=The Billings Gazette |location=Billings, MT|page=2 |date=September 28, 1927 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163388/bahai_mary_hanford_fordpictured/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title =Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford ...|newspaper =Kansas City Star |location=Kansas City, MO |page=3 |date =August 11, 1927 }}</ref> But by October she was noted in New York talking at a race amity meeting.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahais Urge Racial Amity at Annual Meet |newspaper=The Chicago Defender |location=Chicago, IL|page=2 |date=October 22, 1927 |url= <!--|access-date=April 18, 2015-->}} * {{cite news |title=Racial Amity Urged |newspaper=The Pittsburgh Courier |location=Pittsburgh, PA|page=3 |date=October 29, 1927 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/269264/the_pittsburgh_courier/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
Ford next appeared in February at another site for the Baháʼí Center - 119 W 57th St.<ref>* {{cite news |title=At the afternoon service tomorrow ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=February 18, 1928 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163392/bahais_j_otis_swift_and_mary_hanford/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=(Three stories) A Fellowship of Faiths meeting will ... (and) At the afternoon service tomorrow ... (and) Baha'i Centre ... |newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=February 18, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale%20-%201720.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=A Fellowship of Faiths meeting ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=February 18, 1928 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163390/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_on_list_of/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Her next several appearances were in April.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=April 21, 1928 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/845155/bahais_mary_hanford_ford_and_hooper/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=April 21, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale%20-%203414.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Free Class ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York NY|page=11 |date=April 28, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%209/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201928%20%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201928%20%20Grayscale%20-%202271.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> April also saw Ford publish another article on the Baháʼí Temple in ''Star of the West'',<ref>{{cite news |title=The Baháʼí Temple |author=Mary Hanford Ford |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=19 |issue=1 |pages=18–22 |date=April 1928 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=19&page=18 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> which was echoed in ''Baháʼí World'' volumes 3 ''and'' 4.<ref name=BWindex/> That September Ford returned to England for three weeks giving talks on the religion,<ref name=Whitehead/> and went further - an International [[Peace Congress]] in Antwerp and then an International [[Esperanto]] Congress in Amsterdam.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs Ford in England |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=3 |date=September 1928 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=198 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=International Congresses use Esperanto - Dear Fellow-workers: ...|author=Martha L. Root |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=19 |issue=8 |page=(see p. 243) |date=November 1928 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=19&page=227 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Disarmament of Minds |author=Martha L. Root |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=19 |issue=9 |pages=270–274 |date=December 1928 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=19&page=254 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Ford returned and gave roughly monthly talks in New York in October,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Free - Four special Baha'i lectures ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=15 |date=October 27, 1928 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163393/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talk_series/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=18 |date=October 27, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20a%20Grayscale%20-%203733.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> November,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai Centre ...|newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=7F |date=November 24, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201928%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201928%20Grayscale%20-%208773.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=7 |date=November 24, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20a%20Grayscale%20-%206271.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and December.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=26 |date=December 22, 1928 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163397/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=A7 |date=December 22, 1928 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201928%20Grayscale%20-%208546.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
January 1929 began with Ford giving a talk at the Baháʼí Center.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=9 |date=January 12, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale%20-%200418.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |page=28 |date=January 26, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%209/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201929%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201929%20Grayscale%20-%201091.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=7 |date=January 26, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale%20-%200983.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Ford also bought a subscription to [[The Crisis]] and communicated briefly with [[W. E. B. DuBois]].<ref>* {{cite web |author=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Letter from Mary H. Ford to W. E. B. Du Bois |work=W. E. B. Du Bois Papers |publisher=Special Collections & University Archives, University of Massachusetts Amherst |date=January 11, 1929 |url=http://credo.library.umass.edu/view/full/mums312-b048-i343 |access-date=May 10, 2015}} * {{cite web |author=W. E. B. Du Bois |title=Letter from W. E. B. Du Bois to Mary H. Ford |work=W. E. B. Du Bois Papers |publisher=Special Collections & University Archives, University of Massachusetts Amherst |date=January 17, 1929 |url=http://credo.library.umass.edu/view/full/mums312-b048-i344 |access-date=May 10, 2015}}</ref> In February Ford continued her monthly talks at the Baha'i Center,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=7 |date=February 2, 1929 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163402/talks_at_bahai_center_archbishop/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=7 |date=February 9, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale%20-%201505.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and March.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=10 |date=March 9, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201929%20Grayscale%20-%202633.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In April she was a guest in Geneva, New York.<ref>{{cite news |title=Personals; Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford of New York City ...|newspaper=Geneva Daily Times |page=9 |date=April 16, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/Geneva%20NY%20Daily%20Times/Geneva%20NY%20Daily%20Times%201929%20Mar-Jun%201929%20Grayscale/Geneva%20NY%20Daily%20Times%201929%20Mar-Jun%201929%20Grayscale%20-%200505.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In May she was on a committee helping to host a "Peace Week" headed up by Edith Claire Bryce Cram of "Peace House"<ref>{{cite news |title=Peace Week Celebration |newspaper=New York Evening Post |page=15 |date=May 2, 1929 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201929%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201929%20Grayscale%20-%202596.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as addressing the national Baháʼí convention in support of the Baháʼí Temple,<ref>{{cite news |title=Report of the Twenty-first annual convention |author=Louis Gregory |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=4 |date=May 1929 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=236 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and wrote her own article covering the convention too.<ref>{{cite news |title=Impressions of the Baha'i Convention |author=Mary Hanford Ford |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=20 |issue=4 |pages=124–125 |date=July 1929 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=20&page=124 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
Ford was seemingly quiet from July until November, perhaps in Europe,<ref name=Italy/> when she again is giving a talk at the Baháʼí Center.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=November 2, 1929 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163408/bahais_mary_hanford_ford_julia/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> There is a break in her efforts again until February 1930 when she again gave talks at the Baha'i Center.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre |newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=9 |date=February 1, 1930 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/1879355/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_give_talks/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=9 |date=February 1, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20Grayscale%20-%201338.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She continued at a rate of one a month in March,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=10 |date=March 1, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930%20-%201365.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and April,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=8 |date=April 5, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20Grayscale%20-%202670.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> though in April she was also scheduled in Madison, Wisconsin.<ref>{{cite news |author=Mrs. F. O. Pennington |title=League of Voters is making a creative raid on indifference |newspaper=Madison Capital Times |page=26 |date=December 31, 1929 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/wisconsin/madison/madison-capital-times/1929/12-31/page-26 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> She also wrote a remembrance of her time at the exhibit of the Baha'i Temple model in New York.<ref name=remmeber/>
She gave talks in May at the Center,<ref>*{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=14 |date=May 3, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930%20-%202948.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=6 |date=May 17, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201930%20-%203547.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and was visible as a signatory of the cablegram<ref>{{cite news |title=Indian Pamphlets call for Revolt (continued from page one) |newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=2 |date=May 10, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale%20-%203223.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> of New York clergy supporting peace in India with the work of [[Mahatma Gandhi|Gandhi]]<ref>{{cite news |title=102 Clergy of U.S. cable MacDonald for peace in India |newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=1 |date=May 10, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale%20-%203222.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and his March [[Salt March]]. And she was noted renting an apartment from lower 2nd Ave.<ref>{{cite news |title=Renting from plans |newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=8F |date=May 31, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201930%20Grayscale%20-%203821.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In June she was listed starting a series of talks in London,<ref>{{cite news |title=Program of meetings - London, England |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=6 |date=July 1930 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=328 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as submitted a paper to an Esperanto conference in August there.<ref>{{cite news |title=A Universal Language - Which? |author=A. Evelyn Baxter |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=21 |issue=9 |pages=254–256 |date=November 1930 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=21&page=254 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Come October Ford is back giving a talk at the Baha'i Center in New York,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=11 |date=October 25, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201930%20a%20Grayscale%20-%202171.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> followed by one in November,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=November 15, 1930 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163424/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and then by one for a race amity conference,<ref>{{cite news |title=Urban League and Baha'is of NY conference |newspaper=The Chicago Defender |location=Chicago, Ill |page=2 |date=November 22, 1930 |url= <!--|access-date=April 18, 2015-->}}</ref> as well as a follow-up at the Baháʼí Center in December.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mrs. Bethune was guest of Foreign Study Club |newspaper=The New York Age |page=2 |date=December 20, 1930 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20NY%20Age/New%20York%20NY%20Age%201929-1930%20%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Age%201929-1930%20%20Grayscale%20-%200998.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
January 1931 began with talks in the Baháʼí Center.<ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=8a |date=January 17, 1931 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201931/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201931%20-%200676.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> After a brief lapse she was visible helping to host meetings like for the Urban League in March,<ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i meeting held at Urban League building |newspaper=The New York Age |location=New York, NY|page=3 |date=March 14, 1931 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/273434/bahai_meeting_at_urban_league/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as at the Baháʼí Center.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=March 21, 1931 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163428/bahais_mary_hanford_ford_and_edgar/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Talks of hers continue into April.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Baha'i - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=57 |date=April 18, 1931 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201931/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201931%20-%204259.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=April 18, 1931 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20Grayscale%20-%204480.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
During that Summer, her fifth in Britain, she spoke at the first public meeting of the religion in [[Bradford, Yorkshire]].<ref name=Whitehead/><ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i activities in other lands; London |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=6 |date=September 1931 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=442 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Then at the end of the summer it was noted that Ford had chaired a group bringing in a diversity of speakers "represented art, music, drama, literature, current events and world peace" to the Baháʼí Center - Jean Anthony reported presenters across about a page of text in the August edition of ''Baháʼí News'', the national newspaper of the religion for decades.<ref name=Whitehead/><ref>{{cite news |title=Afternoon Tea Talks at the New York Baha'i Center |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=7 |date=August 1931 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=435 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> An article "The Baháʼí Temple" was published in ''Baháʼí World'' vol 3 published 1931.<ref name=BW3>[https://s3.amazonaws.com/bahaiworld/BW_Volume3.pdf The Baháʼí World(big download)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304052938/https://s3.amazonaws.com/bahaiworld/BW_Volume3.pdf |date=March 4, 2016 }}, Baha'i World, Volume 3, BE 85–86, 1928-1930AD, published 1931: The Baháʼí Temple by Mary Hanford Ford (pp. 151–154), The Mashriqu'l-Adhkar in America by Shahnaz Waite (pp. 142–149, see pp 144–5, 147)</ref> It includes mentions of Ford's work at the Kevorkian Gallery in New York and the Art Institute in Chicago presenting the model of the Temple (note the article itself includes an excerpt from the article "Symbolism of the Baháʼí Temple" Ford wrote).
Ford re-appeared in New York in October giving a talk at the Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=October 17, 1931 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20a%20Grayscale%20-%202003.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and then in November.<ref>* {{cite news |title = Mary Handford Ford, ...|newspaper =Asbury Park Press |location=Asbury Park, NJ|page =15 |date =November 7, 1931 |url =https://www.newspapers.com/clip/12879705/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_talks_at_home/|access-date = August 5, 2017 }} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=11 |date=November 14, 1931 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201931%20a%20Grayscale%20-%203402.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In New York meetings at the Baháʼí Center continued on Sundays through the year.<ref name=NY/> In February Ford spoke at another reception held by the Baháʼís in honor of the [[National Association for the Advancement of Colored People]] and the New York [[Urban League]] as well as at Esperantist conferences.<ref name=HolleySurvey>{{cite book |author=Horace Holley |author-link=Horace Holley (Baháʼí) |title=Baha'i World |chapter=Survey of current Baha'i activities in the East and the West |volume=7 |pages=60–61, 64, 70, 71, 73 |date=1935 |chapter-url=http://www.h-net.org/~bahai/diglib/articles/F-J/holley/Survey_of_Current_Baha'i_Activities_1930-1932.pdf |access-date=May 11, 2015}}1935?</ref> Additionally, still in February, Ford gave a talk in New Jersey.<ref>{{cite news |title=New Jersey |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=4 |date=March 1932 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=476 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In April Ford gave more than one talk at the Center.<ref>* {{cite news |title=E. B. Kinney will speak tomorrow ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=15 |date=April 2, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201932%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201932%20Grayscale%20-%203913.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Dr. Genevieve Coy will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=April 9, 1932 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163431/bahais_genevieve_coy_and_mary_hanford/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=April 16, 1932 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163435/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Still in April Ford was elected president of a vegetarian association in New York.<ref>{{cite news |title=The vegetarian Faith to thrive and convince |newspaper=Chicago Packer |location=Chicago, IL|page=14 |date=April 23, 1932 |url=http://idnc.library.illinois.edu/cgi-bin/illinois?a=d&d=CHP19320423.2.163 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In May Ford spoke at a youth conference of a church group,<ref>{{cite news |title=Youth to hear its task outlined at conference at St. Andrew's |newspaper=The Herald Statesman |location=Yonkers, NY|page=12 |date=May 11, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2010/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale%20-%200179.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as at another church group,<ref>{{cite news |title=Dance is 'Divine' expression, Ruth St. Denis tells youth |newspaper=The Herald Statesman |location=Yonkers, NY|page=2 |date=May 21, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2010/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale%20-%200495.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
The British national assembly noted that in addition to her work in America among the 17 noted Baháʼís undertaking activities to promote the religion in the UK, she was singled out as being responsible for "the most outstanding teaching activity during the year".<ref name=HolleySurvey/> Ford also spoke a few days after a Women's Peace Crusade at a Baháʼí follow-up.<ref>{{cite news |title=Wings over Europe |author=Florence E. Pinchon |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=23 |issue=8 |page=250 |date=November 1932 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=23&page=250 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
Ford was next visible in October when she gives a series of talks at the Center in New York.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=October 15, 1932 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163437/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=October 29, 1932 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163439/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=October 29, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201932%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201932%20a%20Grayscale%20-%202808.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In November she attended a banquet,<ref>{{cite news |title=Holiday services in many Churches; ... Mass meeting at Union Auditorium ...|newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=10F |date=November 19, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201932%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201932%20Grayscale%20-%208419.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> a meeting of the All-World Gandhi Fellowship,<ref>{{cite news |title=All-World Gandhi Fellowship |newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=15 |date=November 21, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201932%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201932%20Grayscale%20-%208446.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and a gave a talk at the Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Pianist is heard at Bahai meeting |newspaper=The Herald Statesman |location=Yonkers, NY|page=4 |date=November 21, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2010/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale%20-%204076.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> as well as another in Yonkers.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai Assembly to hear Mrs. Ford |newspaper=The Herald Statesman |location=Yonkers, NY|page=16 |date=November 29, 1932 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2010/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201932%20Grayscale%20-%204211.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In December Ford again spoke at the Center.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=17 |date=December 24, 1932 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163447/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
==New England and the Central States in her last years==
In January 1933 monthly talks by Ford began with a regional conference of five communities held in [[Yonkers, New York]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai Assemblies gather tomorrow for session here |newspaper=The Herald Statesman |location=Yonkers, NY|page=12 |date=January 27, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2010/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201933%20Grayscale/Yonkers%20NY%20Herald%20Statesman%201933%20Grayscale%20-%200366.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In February she spoke at the Baháʼí Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=A New Social Order |newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=10 |date=February 18, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933%20-%201549.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> March at a chapter of the Women's International League for Peace,<ref>{{cite news |title=Peace League to hear Bahai cult member |newspaper=Long Island Daily Press |location=Long Island, NY|page=10 |date=March 10, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2014/Jamaica%20NY%20Long%20Island%20Daily%20Press/Jamaica%20NY%20Long%20Island%20Daily%20Press%201933/Jamaica%20NY%20Long%20Island%20Daily%20Press%201933%20-%201403.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> April to the All-Gandhi World Fellowship,<ref>{{cite news |title=Testimonial luncheon to Swami Bodhananda ...|newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=14 F |date=April 22, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201933%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201933%20Grayscale%20-%202243.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and then back at the Baháʼí Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i ...|newspaper=New York Evening Post |location=New York, NY|page=9F |date=April 29, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2011/New%20York%20Evening%20Post/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201933%20Grayscale/New%20York%20NY%20Evening%20Post%201933%20Grayscale%20-%202408.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> May at the "World's Good Will Day" observance,<ref>{{cite news |title=Good will day being observed at meeting |newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=29 |date=May 18, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933%20-%204525.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> June back at the Baháʼí Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=6 |date=June 3, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201933%20-%205057.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and in July,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=9 |date=July 15, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201933%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201933%20Grayscale%20-%208355.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> before going on to give talks at Green Acre later in July,<ref>{{cite news |title=Green Acre Notes |newspaper=Portsmouth Herald |location=Portsmouth, NH|page=3 |date=July 25, 1933 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/12891782/talks_at_green_acre_harlan_ober_mary/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and a Race Amity conference there in August,<ref>{{cite news |title=Conference at Green Acre great success |newspaper=Portsmouth Herald |location=Portsmouth, NH|page=3 |date=August 1, 1933 |url=https://newspaperarchive.com/us/new-hampshire/portsmouth/portsmouth-herald/1933/08-01/page-3 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> which is her last known appearance there. It was in July that she published recollections of her pilgrimage,<ref name=SoW24.4/> and this race amity conference was noted in the ''Baháʼí News'' as well.<ref>{{cite news |title=Important Inter-Racial Amity Activities ...|newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=4 |date=September 1933 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=620 |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=New Visions of Human Unity |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=11 |date=February 1934 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=662 |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref> In August/September attended the 1933 World Parliament of Religions.<ref>{{cite thesis |author=Carlos Hugo Parra |title=Standing with Unfamiliar Company on Uncommon Ground: The Catholic Church and the Chicago Parliaments of Religions |page=142 |publisher=Education Ontario Institute for Studies in Education University of Toronto |date=2012 |url=https://tspace.library.utoronto.ca/bitstream/1807/34835/1/Parra_Carlos_H_201211_PhD_thesis.pdf |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref> In October Ford was at the [[Chicago Century of Progress Exposition]] as one of the representatives of the national community,<ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i Representation at World Fellowship of Faiths |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=3 |date=October 1933 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=623 |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref> as well as at a local community meeting in [[Toledo, Ohio]].<ref>{{cite news |title=The Toledo, Ohio, Spiritual Assembly ...|newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=6 |date=October 1933 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=626 |access-date=May 23, 2015}}</ref> In November she helped celebrate the Baháʼí Holy Day of the Birth of Baháʼu'lláh at the Baháʼí Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Hooper Harris, Mary Hanford Ford and ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn NY|page=11 |date=November 11, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201933%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201933%20a%20Grayscale%20-%202314.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and then gave a talk another day.<ref>{{cite news |title=David Eugene Smith, Horace Holley ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=11 |date=November 25, 1933 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163460/bahais_david_eugene_smith_horace/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In December she gave talks in [[Binghamton, New York]] a few nights<ref>* {{cite news |title=World Bahai lecturer to give lecture here |newspaper=Binghamton Press |location=Binghamton, NY|page=13 |date=December 5, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201933/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201933%20-%207368.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mrs. Ford will speak Friday night on 'Jesus and Bahai Revelation' |newspaper=Binghamton Press |location=Binghamton, NY|page=3 |date=December 6, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201933/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201933%20-%207382.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford is the guest ...|newspaper=The Bringhamton Press |location=Binghamton, NY|page=4 |date=December 9, 1933 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934.pdf/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934%20-%200077.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> December also saw her publish an article on a portrait of ʻAbdu'l-Baha in ''Star of the West'',<ref>{{cite news |title=The Portrait of ʻAbdu'l-Baha |author=Mary Hanford Ford |newspaper=Star of the West |volume=24 |issue=9 |pages=276–277 |date=December 1933 |url=http://starofthewest.info/viewer.erb?vol=24&page=276 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> which was echoed in ''Baháʼí World'' vol 5.<ref name=BWindex/>
Early January 1934 began with a talk at the Center,<ref>{{cite news |title="The Bab - His Spiritual Message" will be ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York, NY|page=31 |date=January 6, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201934/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201934%20-%200231.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and a week later.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Bahai Message explained by ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=28 |date=January 11, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20Grayscale%20-%200369.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In February Ford was with Ali Kuli Khan in [[Urbana, Illinois]] giving a series of talks,<ref>{{cite news |title=Urbana |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=8 |date=February 1934 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=659 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> followed by a visit with a presentation in Chicago and the Temple there.<ref>* {{cite news |title=Chicago |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=8 |date=February 1934 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=659 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Temple Program |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=5 |date=January 1935 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=725 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> In April Ford was back giving a monthly talk in the New York Baháʼí Center,<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=April 14, 1934 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163461/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Current Activities: The New York Assembly ...|newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=5 |date=September 1934 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=707 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> then in May,<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=6 |date=May 5, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20Grayscale%20-%204915.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and in June.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=5 |date=June 2, 1934 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163464/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
That summer Ford was visible starting with Summer School classes and talks in late June for youth in the Central States{{where|date=June 2015}} followed by a general program into July.<ref>{{cite news |title=Central States Summer Schools |newspaper=Baháʼí News |pages=4–5 |date=April 1935 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=748 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> Later Ford was in [[Milwaukee, Wisconsin]] in late September or early October,<ref>{{cite news |title=Milwaukee Public Meetings |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=11 |date=January 1935 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=731 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> and Binghamton, New York in November for a few talks,<ref>* {{cite news |title=Binghamton |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=11 |date=March 1935 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=743 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai teacher to speak in hotel here on Friday |newspaper=Binghamton Press |location=Binghamton, NY|page=7 |date=November 6, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934.pdf/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934%20-%206926.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mrs. Mary Hanford Ford will speak here tonight |newspaper=Binghamptom Press |location=Binghamptom, NY|page=3 |date=November 9, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934.pdf/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934%20-%207016.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai movement still continues, asserts speakers |newspaper=Binghamton Press |location=Binghamptom, NY|page=11 |date=November 10, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper4/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934.pdf/Binghamton%20NY%20Press%20Grayscale%201934%20-%207066.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> then [[Syracuse, New York]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Bahai woman hits Capitalism |newspaper=Syracuse Journal |location=Syracuse, NY|page=20 |date=November 12, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2017/Syracuse%20NY%20Journal/Syracuse%20NY%20Journal%201934/Syracuse%20NY%20Journal%201934%20-%208819.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> before returning to New York City area and its Center before the end of November.<ref>{{cite news |title=NY|newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=16 |date=April 1935 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=760 |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=At the meeting of the Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=The New York Sun |location=New York NY|page=12 |date=November 24, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%2018/New%20York%20NY%20Sun/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201934/New%20York%20NY%20Sun%201934%20-%209715.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Bahai - Baha'i Centre ...|newspaper=Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=8 |date=December 1, 1934 |url=http://fultonhistory.com/Newspaper%205/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20Grayscale/Brooklyn%20NY%20Daily%20Eagle%201934%20a%20Grayscale%20-%202757.pdf |access-date=April 18, 2015}} * {{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=December 22, 1934 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163467/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
She doesn't appear at the New York Center after December 1934 until April 1935.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mary Hanford Ford will speak ...|newspaper=The Brooklyn Daily Eagle |location=Brooklyn, NY|page=10 |date=April 6, 1935 |url=https://www.newspapers.com/clip/2163468/bahai_mary_hanford_ford_gives_talk/ |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
For all the remarkable coverage of decades of appearances there are no known records of her after April 1935 until her death in 1937. She probably moved to Toledo, Ohio where her daughter lived.<ref name=Truher/> The next entry in the ''Baháʼí News'' is notice of her death.<ref>{{cite news |title=In Memoriam |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=3 |date=March 1937 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=01&page=902 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref> However she had been working on a book on the religion and facing the issue of industrial work conditions.<ref name=Whitehead/> She died in [[Clearwater, Florida]] on February 2, 1937, with her daughter reporting her last words: "It is so beautiful, Lynette, it is so very beautiful."<ref name=DLH/>
==Legacy==
At a memorial for her a few days later in New York Ali Kuli Khan read a cable from then head of the religion, [[Shoghi Effendi]]:
<blockquote>Her unique and outstanding gifts enabled her to promote effectively the best interests of the Faith in its new-born and divinely-conceived institutions. I will pray for her soul from the depths of my heart. Her services will always be remembered and extolled.<ref name=Whitehead/></blockquote>
In 1944 Philip Leonard Green recalls encountering the religion by contact from Ford and her gift of her book "The World of Abdu'l-Baha".<ref name=Philip/>
In 1947 in Italy a niece of a lady who had met Ford in 1910 was found - she recalled her aunt, Mrs. Max Schobert, had her two talks from Ford in 1910 and then met ʻAbdu'l-Bahá.<ref>{{cite news |title=Italy (Condensation of a letter from Rome by Ugo Giachery) |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=10 |date=October 1947 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=02&page=1058 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In 1950 the Baháʼís of Washington state asked the national assembly to re-issue Ford's ''Oriental Rose''.<ref>{{cite news |title=Washington (decisions of the national assembly from reports of state conventions) |newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=4 |date=May 1950 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=03&page=266 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In 1965 Ford's daughter Lynette Storm continued in the religion after her mother and was photographed with Margaret Ruhe commenting that her mother was at the 1893 Exposition and that she thought her mother had run into the religion there.<ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i in the News; Mrs Lynette Storm (left), Toledo, Ohio ...|newspaper=Baháʼí News |page=15 |date=August 1965 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=06&page=207 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Baha'i Faith unites diverse groups |author=Eugene Gauger |newspaper=Toledo Blade |location=Toledo, OH|page=8 |date=June 4, 1965 |url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=1350&dat=19650604&id=E-pOAAAAIBAJ&pg=7422,2236852&hl=en |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In 1972 an excerpt of Ford's ''Oriental Rose'' was included in Annamarie K. Hannold's Glimpig Early Baháʼí Pilgrimages.<ref>{{cite news |title=Pilgrimage began in the days of Baháʼu'lláh |newspaper=Baháʼí News |pages=2–3 |date=October 1972 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=08&page=194 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
In 1974 Bruce Whitemore published ''The Silent Teacher'' and noted her ''The Baháʼí Temple'' article in Baháʼí World vol iii.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Silent Teacher (footnotes) |author=Bruce Whitmore |newspaper=Baháʼí News |pages=6–16 (see p. 16) |date=November 1974 |url=http://bahai-news.info/viewer.erb?vol=08&page=809 |access-date=April 18, 2015}}</ref>
From Marzieh Gail's 1991 book, ''Arches of Years'', "Mrs Ford was actually a premature 'sixties person', as her life and opinions clearly show. ... She, who had known poverty herself, stood for labor and the underdog. She, an intellectual, stood for beauty, art, the life of the mind."<ref name=Arches/>
Ford's 1889 critique, ''A Feminine Iconoclast'', originally published in ''The Nationalist'' was reprinted in Carol Farley Kessler's 1995 book, ''Daring to Dream: Utopian Fiction by United States Women Before, 1950''.<ref name=Daring>{{cite book |author=Carol Farley Kessler |title=Daring to Dream: Utopian Fiction by United States Women Before, 1950 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=a1drkz2UrPoC&pg=PA104 |date=January 1995 |publisher=Syracuse University Press |isbn=978-0-8156-2655-8 |pages=104–108}}</ref>

Copyright Office |title=Catalog of Copyright Entries |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=qNFGAQAAMAAJ&pg=RA1-PA38 |year=1891 |publisher=U.S. Government Printing Office |page=14}}</ref> * {{cite book |author=Mary Hanford Finney Ford |title=Which Wins?: A Story of Social Conditions |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=8d0vAQAAMAAJ |year=1891 |publisher=Lee and Shepard}} * {{cite book |author=Mary Hanford Ford |title=Otto's Inspiration |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=d6M_AAAAYAAJ |year=1895 |publisher=S. {{Short description|American politician and businessman (born 1947)}} {{About|the American politician|the football player who went by the same name|Milton Romney|the singer with a similar sounding name|Ritt Momney}} {{Featured article}} {{Pp-move|small=yes}} {{Pp-blp|small=yes}} {{Use American English|date=February 2019}} {{Use mdy dates|date=December 2023}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Mitt Romney | image = Mitt Romney official US Senate portrait.jpg | caption = Official portrait, 2019 | alt = Head shot of Romney smiling | jr/sr = United States Senator | state = [[Utah]] | term_start = January 3, 2019 | term_end = January 3, 2025 | predecessor = [[Orrin Hatch]] | successor = [[John Curtis]] | order1 = 70th [[Governor of Massachusetts]] | lieutenant1 = [[Kerry Healey]] | term_start1 = January 2, 2003 | term_end1 = January 4, 2007 | predecessor1 = [[Jane Swift]] (acting) | successor1 = [[Deval Patrick]] | office2 = [[President of the Organising Committee for the Olympic Games|President]] of the [[Salt Lake Organizing Committee for the Olympic and Paralympic Winter Games of 2002]] | president2 = [[Juan Antonio Samaranch]]
[[Jacques Rogge]] | term_start2 = February 12, 1999 | term_end2 = August 2002 | predecessor2 = [[James L. Easton]] | successor2 = [[Valentino Castellani]] | birth_name = Willard Mitt Romney | birth_date = {{birth date and age|1947|3|12}} | birth_place = [[Detroit]], Michigan, U.S. | party = [[Independent politician|Independent]] (before 1993)
[[Republican Party (United States)|Republican]] (1993–present) | spouse = {{marriage|[[Ann Romney|Ann Davies]]|March 21, 1969}} | children = 5, including [[Tagg Romney|Tagg]] | parents = {{ubl |[[George W. Romney]] |[[Lenore Romney|Lenore LaFount Romney]]}} | relatives = [[Romney family]] | education = {{ubl |[[Stanford University]] (attended) |[[Brigham Young University]] ([[Bachelor of Arts|BA]]) |[[Harvard University]] ([[Juris Doctor|JD]], [[Master of Business Administration|MBA]])}} | signature = Mitt Romney Signature.svg | signature_alt = Cursive signature in ink |module = {{Listen |pos = center |embed = yes |filename = Mitt Romney's remarks urging his colleagues not to object to the certification of the election results following the January 6th Insurrection.oga |title = Romney's voice |type = speech |description = Romney on [[2021 United States Electoral College vote count#Announcements of planned Electoral College vote count objections|objections to certifying the electoral results]] following the [[January 6 United States Capitol attack|January 6 Capitol attack]]
Recorded January 6, 2021}} }} {{Mitt Romney series}} '''Willard Mitt Romney''' (born March 12, 1947) is an American businessman and retired politician who served as a [[United States Senate|United States senator]] from [[Utah]] from 2019 to 2025 and as the 70th [[governor of Massachusetts]] from 2003 to 2007. He was the [[Republican Party (United States)|Republican Party]]'s nominee in the [[2012 United States presidential election|2012 U.S. presidential election]]. Mitt Romney is a son of [[George W. Romney]], a former governor of [[Michigan]]. Raised in [[Bloomfield Hills, Michigan]], Mitt spent over two years in France as a [[Mormon missionary]]. He married [[Ann Romney|Ann Davies]] in 1969; they have five sons. Active in [[the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints]] (LDS Church) throughout his adult life, Romney served as [[Bishop (Latter Day Saints)|bishop]] of his [[Ward (LDS Church)|ward]] and later as a [[Stake (LDS Church)|stake president]] for an area covering Boston and many of its suburbs. By 1971, he had participated in the political campaigns of both his parents. In 1971, Romney graduated with a [[Bachelor of Arts]] in [[English studies|English]] from [[Brigham Young University]] (BYU) and in 1975 he completed a [[JD–MBA]] program from [[Harvard University|Harvard]]. He became a management consultant and in 1977 joined [[Bain & Company]] in [[Boston]]. As Bain's [[chief executive officer]] (CEO), he helped lead the company out of a financial crisis. In 1984, he co-founded and led the spin-off company [[Bain Capital]], a [[private equity]] investment firm that became one of the [[List of private equity firms|largest of its kind]] in the nation. After stepping down from his positions at Bain Capital and in the LDS Church, Romney ran as the Republican nominee for the [[U.S. Senate]] in Massachusetts in [[1994 United States Senate election in Massachusetts|1994]] and lost to the incumbent, [[Ted Kennedy]]. He then resumed his position at Bain Capital. Years later, a successful stint as president and CEO of the then-struggling [[Salt Lake Organizing Committee for the Olympic and Paralympic Winter Games of 2002|Salt Lake Organizing Committee]] for the [[2002 Winter Olympics]] led to a relaunch of his political career. Elected governor of Massachusetts in [[2002 Massachusetts gubernatorial election|2002]], Romney helped develop and later signed a [[Massachusetts health care reform|health care reform]] law (commonly called "Romneycare") that provided near-universal health insurance access through state-level subsidies and [[health insurance mandate|individual mandates to purchase insurance]]. He also presided over the elimination of a projected $1.2–1.5 billion deficit through a combination of spending cuts, increased fees, and closing corporate [[tax loopholes]]. Romney did not seek reelection in [[2006 Massachusetts gubernatorial election|2006]], instead focusing on his campaign [[2008 Republican Party presidential primaries|for the Republican nomination in the 2008 presidential election]], which he lost to Senator [[John McCain]]. Romney ran for president again four years later and was the Republican nominee in the [[2012 Republican Party presidential primaries|2012 presidential election]], becoming the first LDS Church member to be a [[major party]]'s nominee. He lost the election to President [[Barack Obama]]. After reestablishing residency in Utah, Romney ran for U.S. Senate in [[2018 United States Senate election in Utah|2018]]. When Romney won the Republican nomination and general election, he became the first person in modern American history to be elected governor and U.S. senator of different states.{{Cite web |last=Schott |first=Bryan |date=September 12, 2017 |title=Romney could be first person to be a Governor and Senator for two different states in more than 100 years |url=https://utahpolicy.com/archive/14304-romney-could-be-first-person-to-be-a-governor-and-senator-for-two-different-states-in-more-than-100-years |access-date=October 2, 2024 |website=Utah Policy}} Generally considered a [[Moderate Republican (modern United States)|moderate or neoconservative Republican]],{{Cite web |date=July 27, 2022 |title=Is Mitt Romney doing a good job in the Senate? More Democrats than Republicans in Utah think so |url=https://www.deseret.com/utah/2022/7/27/23280627/mitt-romney-job-approval-rating-senate-washington-centrist-moderate-bipartisan-utah-poll |access-date=August 20, 2022 |website=[[Deseret News]] |language=en |archive-date=August 20, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220820172201/https://www.deseret.com/utah/2022/7/27/23280627/mitt-romney-job-approval-rating-senate-washington-centrist-moderate-bipartisan-utah-poll |url-status=live}}{{Cite web |date=February 3, 2022 |title=Are Republicans showing Mitt Romney more love? New Utah poll has the answers |url=https://www.deseret.com/utah/2022/2/2/22913085/do-republicans-like-mitt-romney-poll-numbers-joe-biden-donald-trump-hinckley-inistitute-utah |access-date=August 20, 2022 |website=[[Deseret News]] |language=en |archive-date=August 20, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220820172200/https://www.deseret.com/utah/2022/2/2/22913085/do-republicans-like-mitt-romney-poll-numbers-joe-biden-donald-trump-hinckley-inistitute-utah |url-status=live}} Romney was the lone Republican to vote to convict [[Donald Trump]] in his [[First impeachment trial of Donald Trump|first impeachment trial]], making him the first senator ever to have voted to remove a president of the same party from office.{{cite web |url=https://www.vox.com/2020/2/5/21125118/mitt-romney-impeachment-vote-history |title=Mitt Romney just did something that literally no senator has ever done before |website=[[Vox (website)|Vox]] |date=February 5, 2020 |access-date=January 1, 2023 |archive-date=January 2, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230102014601/https://www.vox.com/2020/2/5/21125118/mitt-romney-impeachment-vote-history |url-status=live}} Romney also voted to convict in Trump's [[Second impeachment trial of Donald Trump|second trial]] in 2021. He marched alongside [[Black Lives Matter]] protestors, voted to [[Ketanji Brown Jackson Supreme Court nomination|confirm Ketanji Brown Jackson]] to the [[Supreme Court of the United States|Supreme Court]], supported [[Bipartisan Safer Communities Act|gun control]] measures, and did not vote for Trump in the [[2016 United States presidential election|2016]], [[2020 United States presidential election|2020]], and [[2024 United States presidential election|2024]] presidential elections. He has long been hawkish on relations with [[Iran–United States relations|Iran]], [[China–United States relations|China]], and [[Russia–United States relations|Russia]], and was one of [[Israel]]'s staunchest supporters in Congress. In 2023, Romney announced he would not run for [[2024 United States Senate election in Utah|reelection in 2024]] and retired from the Senate when his term expired in 2025.{{Cite news |last=Balz |first=Dan |date=September 13, 2023 |title=Mitt Romney says he will not seek a second term in the Senate |language=en-US |newspaper=Washington Post |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/09/13/mitt-romney-senate-reelection/ |access-date=September 13, 2023 |issn=0190-8286 |archive-date=September 13, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230913213815/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/09/13/mitt-romney-senate-reelection/ |url-status=live}} ==Early and personal life== ===Heritage and youth=== {{See also|Romney family}} Willard Mitt Romney{{cite news |url=https://www.chicagotribune.com/2012/05/29/romneys-birth-certificate-evokes-his-fathers-controversy/ |title=Romney's birth certificate evokes his father's controversy |first=Mark |last=Hosenball |agency=[[Reuters]] |newspaper=[[Chicago Tribune]] |date=May 29, 2012 |access-date=April 19, 2015 |archive-date=October 20, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020083249/http://articles.chicagotribune.com/2012-05-29/news/sns-rt-us-usa-campaign-romney-birth-certificatebre84s1gf-20120529_1_george-romney-republican-mitt-romney-citizen |url-status=live}} Also see [http://static.reuters.com/resources/media/editorial/20120529/RomneyBirthCertificate.pdf "State of Michigan Certificate of Live Birth"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230315085751/http://static.reuters.com/resources/media/editorial/20120529/RomneyBirthCertificate.pdf |date=March 15, 2023 }}. was born on March 12, 1947, at [[Harper University Hospital]] in [[Detroit]], Michigan,{{cite news |url=https://usatoday30.usatoday.com/news/politics/story/2012-02-17/michigan-primary-santorum-romney/53159656/1 |title=Home sweet home? Michigan primary a challenge for Romney |first=Susan |last=Page |newspaper=[[USA Today]] |date=February 20, 2012 |access-date=February 7, 2020 |archive-date=January 13, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113083727/http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/news/politics/story/2012-02-17/michigan-primary-santorum-romney/53159656/1 |url-status=live}} one of four children born to automobile executive [[George W. Romney]] and actress and homemaker [[Lenore Romney]] (née LaFount).Mahoney, ''The Story of George Romney'', pp. 59–62, 104, 113. His mother was a native of [[Logan, Utah]], and his father was born to American parents in a [[Mormon colonies in Mexico|Mormon colony]] in [[Chihuahua (state)|Chihuahua]], Mexico.{{cite news |title=In besieged Mormon colony, Mitt Romney's Mexican roots |last=Miroff |first=Nick |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=July 21, 2011 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/in-besieged-mormon-colony-mitt-romneys-mexican-roots/2011/07/21/gIQAFGOXVI_story.html |access-date=September 5, 2017 |archive-date=May 18, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210518123356/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/in-besieged-mormon-colony-mitt-romneys-mexican-roots/2011/07/21/gIQAFGOXVI_story.html |url-status=live}}{{cite news |title=Mexican Cousins Keep Romney's Family Tree Rooted |author=Burnett, John |url=http://app1.kuhf.org/articles/npr1327244449-Mexican-Cousins-Keep-Romneys-Family-Tree-Rooted.html |publisher=[[NPR]] |date=January 22, 2012 |access-date=January 27, 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120605053609/http://app1.kuhf.org/articles/npr1327244449-Mexican-Cousins-Keep-Romneys-Family-Tree-Rooted.html |archive-date=June 5, 2012 |url-status=dead}} Of primarily English descent, he also has Scottish and German ancestry.Mahoney, ''The Story of George Romney'', pp. 52, 70.{{Cite book |last=Roberts |first=Gary Boyd |author-link=Gary Boyd Roberts |title=Notable Kin: An Anthology of Columns First Published in the NEHGS NEXUS 1986–1995, Volume 2 |publisher=Carl Boyer, 3rd |year=1998 |location=Boston |page=212 |isbn=978-0-936124-20-9}}{{Cite news |last=Potter |first=Mitch |title=Mitt Romney has Canadian roots |newspaper=[[Toronto Star]] |date=January 23, 2012 |url=https://www.thestar.com/news/world/2012/01/23/mitt_romney_has_canadian_roots.html |access-date=September 5, 2017 |archive-date=March 4, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180304054655/https://www.thestar.com/news/world/2012/01/23/mitt_romney_has_canadian_roots.html |url-status=live}} A fifth-generation member of the LDS Church, he is a great-grandson of [[Miles Park Romney]] and a great-great-grandson of [[Miles Romney]], who converted to Mormonism in its first decade. Another great-great-grandfather, [[Parley P. Pratt]], helped [[Quorum of the Twelve|lead the early church]].{{Cite news |url=http://archive.boston.com/news/nation/articles/2007/06/25/centered_in_faith_a_family_emerges/ |title=The Making of Mitt Romney: Part 2: Centered in faith, a family emerges |author=Kranish, Michael |author2=Paulson, Michael |newspaper=[[The Boston Globe]] |date=June 25, 2007 |author-link=Michael Kranish |author-link2=Michael Paulson |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113083534/http://archive.boston.com/news/nation/articles/2007/06/25/centered_in_faith_a_family_emerges/ |archive-date=January 13, 2020 |access-date=February 7, 2020 |url-status=dead}} Also available as [https://www.deseret.com/2007/7/2/20027702/mitt-s-lds-roots-run-deep "Mitt's LDS roots run deep"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200206173241/https://www.deseret.com/2007/7/2/20027702/mitt-s-lds-roots-run-deep |date=February 6, 2020 }}, ''Deseret Morning News'', July 2, 2007.{{cite news |url=https://www.huffpost.com/entry/mitt-romney-mormon-faith_n_1834466 |title=Mitt Romney Holds Mormon Faith Close Through Political Rise |first=Jaweed |last=Kaleem |work=[[HuffPost]] |date=August 29, 2012 |access-date=February 6, 2020 |archive-date=April 10, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200410060815/https://www.huffpost.com/entry/mitt-romney-mormon-faith_n_1834466 |url-status=live}} [[File:Mitt Romney in 1965 Brook.jpg|thumb|upright=.75|alt=Black and white headshot of a young Romney.|Romney in [[Cranbrook Schools|Cranbrook School]]'s 1965 yearbook]] Romney has three older siblings: Margo, Jane, and Scott.Mahoney, ''The Story of George Romney'', pp. 104, 113. He was named after a family friend, businessman [[J. Willard Marriott]], and his father's cousin, [[Milton Romney|Milton "Mitt" Romney]], a former quarterback for the [[Chicago Bears]].Kranish; Helman, ''The Real Romney'', pp. 14–15. Romney was called "Billy" until kindergarten, when he expressed a preference for "Mitt".{{Cite news |url=https://www.thecrimson.com/article/1994/10/21/romney-gains-momentum-as-he-keeps/ |title=Romney Gains Momentum As He Keeps On Running |author=Gell, Jeffrey N. |newspaper=[[The Harvard Crimson]] |date=October 21, 1994 |access-date=February 7, 2020 |archive-date=January 13, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200113083251/https://www.thecrimson.com/article/1994/10/21/romney-gains-momentum-as-he-keeps/ |url-status=live}} In 1953, the family moved from Detroit to the affluent suburb of [[Bloomfield Hills, Michigan|Bloomfield Hills]] and his father became the chairman and CEO of [[American Motors]] the following year and helped the company avoid bankruptcy and return to profitability.Kranish; Helman, ''The Real Romney'', pp. 15–16. By 1959, his father had become a nationally known figure in print and on television,{{cite book |title=Current Biography Yearbook 1958 |editor-last=Candee |editor-first=Marjorie Dent |publisher=[[H. W. Wilson Company]] |location=New York |year=1958 |page=[https://archive.org/details/currentbiogr195800cand/page/368 368] |isbn=978-0-8242-0124-1 |title-link=Current Biography Yearbook}} and Mitt idolized him. Romney attended public [[elementary school]]s until seventh grade, when he enrolled as one of only a few [[Mormons|Mormon]] students at [[Cranbrook Schools|Cranbrook School]], a private upscale boys' [[University-preparatory school|preparatory school]] a few miles from his home. Many students there came from backgrounds even more privileged than his. Not particularly athletic, he also did not distinguish himself academically.
